mantra:satyanarayan:dhyanam.meaning: You close your eyes and picture Lord Vishnu in your mind — the Lord dressed in bright yellow robes and adorned with jewels, master of the three worlds, who brings joy to every heart. This opening meditation settles your mind on him before the worship begins.

mantra:satyanarayan:avahanam.meaning: You are formally inviting the Lord into your home for the ceremony: 'Dear Lord, please come with Goddess Lakshmi and accept the worship I have prepared for you with love.'

mantra:satyanarayan:asanam.meaning: You are offering the Lord a seat — imagining a beautiful throne of gold and gems — so he can sit comfortably as your honoured guest.

mantra:satyanarayan:padyam.meaning: You are washing the Lord's feet with clean water, a gesture of deep respect for a guest, and asking him to bring peace and happiness to your whole family.

mantra:satyanarayan:panchamrita-snanam.meaning: You are bathing the Lord with the five sacred foods — milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar — as a way of thanking him for the nourishment and nature around you.

mantra:satyanarayan:vastram.meaning: After the bath, you are offering the Lord fresh clothes to wear, giving him your very best.

mantra:satyanarayan:naivedyam.meaning: You are offering the Lord sweet food made with ghee and sugar, and asking him to bless it so that it gives your family strength and health.

mantra:satyanarayan:aarti.meaning: You are waving a lit lamp before the Lord to honour him, asking him to always look on you with love and keep your life full of light.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-d.meaning: You close your eyes and picture Lord Vishnu in your mind — the Lord dressed in bright yellow robes and adorned with jewels, master of the three worlds, who brings joy to every heart. This opening meditation settles your mind on him before the worship begins.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-a.meaning: You are formally inviting the Lord into your home for the ceremony: 'Dear Lord, please come with Goddess Lakshmi and accept the worship I have prepared for you with love.'
You are offering the Lord a seat — imagining a beautiful throne of gold and gems — so he can sit comfortably as your honoured guest.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-p.meaning: You are washing the Lord's feet with clean water, a gesture of deep respect for a guest, and asking him to bring peace and happiness to your whole family.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-a.meaning: A formula offering the arghya, the water of welcome: 'Salutations to the Lord of the senses, whose form is both seen and unseen — please accept this water I offer to you with devotion.'
A formula offering achamana, a little water for the Lord to sip: 'May this pure, sin-cleansing water — imagined as the holy water of the Ganga — be accepted; O Lord, please sip it.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-p.meaning: You are bathing the Lord with the five sacred foods — milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar — as a way of thanking him for the nourishment and nature around you.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-s.meaning: A formula for the clean-water bath: 'This pure, auspicious water gathered from many holy places — O Lord, please accept it for your bath.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-v.meaning: After the bath, you are offering the Lord fresh clothes to wear, giving him your very best.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-y.meaning: A formula offering the sacred thread (yajnopavita): 'Accept this fine sacred thread, O Lord Vishnu, and grant every good thing I wish for.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-c.meaning: A formula offering sandalwood paste: 'O best of gods, please accept this divine, fragrant sandalwood paste to anoint you.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-p.meaning: A formula offering flowers: 'O Lord, please accept these fragrant flowers and garlands I have brought for your worship.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-d.meaning: A formula offering incense: 'Please accept this fragrant incense, worthy to be enjoyed by all the gods.'
A formula offering the lamp: 'Accept this ghee lamp I have lit for you, O Lord of gods, and grant me well-being.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-n.meaning: You are offering the Lord sweet food made with ghee and sugar, and asking him to bless it so that it gives your family strength and health.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-t.meaning: A formula offering tambula (betel leaf with cloves, camphor and cardamom): 'Please accept this betel offering, honoured by the gods, with pleasure.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-p.meaning: A formula offering fruit: 'O Lord, I place this fruit before you; through it, may my life bear good fruit birth after birth.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-a.meaning: You are waving a lit lamp before the Lord to honour him, asking him to always look on you with love and keep your life full of light.

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-p.meaning: A prayer said while circling the Lord: 'Whatever wrongs I have done in past lives, may they all be undone with each step of this circumambulation.'

mantra:satyanarayan:shri-satyanarayana-m.meaning: The closing offering of flowers, with an apology for any mistakes: 'Please graciously accept the leaf, flower, fruit, water and food I have offered. O Lord, though my worship may lack the right words, rites or devotion, let it be counted as complete — may Lord Vishnu be pleased with it.'

mantra:satyanarayan:main-mantra.meaning: The core salutation of the puja: 'Om, I bow to Lord Vasudeva (Vishnu).' It is a simple act of surrender, offering your reverence to the Lord who protects the universe.

mantra:satyanarayan:satyanarayana-namah-.meaning: A short name-salutation: 'Om, salutations to Lord Satyanarayana' — Vishnu worshipped as the very form of truth. It is used to call on him and honour him by name.

mantra:satyanarayan:opening-invocation.meaning: The opening salutation that begins the worship: 'Om, I bow to Lord Vasudeva (Vishnu),' offering your reverence to him as you start the puja.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-achamana-ma.meaning: A preliminary self-purification: the worshipper purifies the principle of the self, of knowledge, and of Shiva within, before beginning the worship.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-sankalpa-ma.meaning: The sankalpa, a formal statement of intent. Naming the cosmic era, place and occasion (with blanks for one's own region, name and family line), the worshipper resolves to perform the worship of Lord Dhanvantari for long life, health and prosperity.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-atma-shodha.meaning: A well-known purification verse: whoever remembers the lotus-eyed Lord (Vishnu), in whatever state of purity or impurity, becomes pure within and without.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-dhyana-mant.meaning: A meditation on Lord Dhanvantari as a four-armed, youthful, lotus-eyed deity clad in yellow, adorned with every ornament, worshipped by gods and demons alike, and holding the pot of amrita (the nectar of immortality).

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-avahana-man.meaning: An invocation welcoming Lord Dhanvantari into the worship: radiant God of gods, honoured by the gods, please come and accept the worship offered to you.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-pushpanjali.meaning: Offers Dhanvantari a seat adorned with gold and jewels, presenting five flowers as his place to be seated.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-swagat-mant.meaning: A simple word of welcome: 'O Lord Dhanvantari, I welcome you.'

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-padya-samar.meaning: Offers water to wash the feet of Dhanvantari, with a salutation to him as lord of the world.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-arghya-sama.meaning: Offers arghya, a respectful offering of water (mixed with sandal, flowers, rice and fruit), saluting Dhanvantari as sustainer of the earth and support of the universe.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-gandha-chan.meaning: Offers fragrant sandal paste to Dhanvantari to anoint his body.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-pushpa-sama.meaning: Offers flowers to Dhanvantari — jasmine, bakula, champak, lotus, punnaga, karavira, bilva, tulsi and mallika among them — praying that he be pleased.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-dhoop-samar.meaning: Offers fragrant incense (dhoop), made from the sap of trees, to Dhanvantari.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-deep-samarp.meaning: Offers a lit ghee lamp to Dhanvantari, a light said to remove the darkness of the three worlds, with a prayer for protection.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-naivedya-sa.meaning: Offers food (naivedya) — sweets, curd, milk and ghee — to Dhanvantari, presented to the five vital breaths of the body (prana, apana, vyana, udana, samana).

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-achamana-ja.meaning: After the food offering, offers water for drinking and sipping (achamana), for washing the hands and face, along with sandal paste for the hands.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-tambula-sam.meaning: Offers tambula (betel leaf with areca nut, camphor and cardamom) to Dhanvantari as a mouth freshener.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-dakshina-ma.meaning: Offers dakshina, a gift of a golden flower, to Dhanvantari, praying for peace and lasting merit.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-pradakshina.meaning: Offers circumambulation to Dhanvantari; the verse says that sins of past births are destroyed with every step taken around the Lord, who is the worshipper's only refuge.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-vandana-sah.meaning: Offers flowers with a prayer for forgiveness: whatever faults have been committed by hand, body, ear, eye or mind, knowingly or unknowingly, please forgive them all.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-sashtanga-p.meaning: Offers the eight-limbed prostration (sashtanga pranam) to Dhanvantari, bowing to the infinite Lord of a thousand forms, with a thousand feet, eyes, heads and arms.

mantra:dhanteras:dhanvantari-kshama-prar.meaning: A closing prayer for pardon: 'I do not know how to invoke you or to conclude the worship, nor the proper rites — forgive me. Whatever worship I have managed, though lacking in mantra, action and devotion, let it be made complete.' It asks Dhanvantari to accept the worshipper as his servant and to grant wellbeing, and dedicates all the worship to him.

mantra:dhanteras:shri-kubera-dhyana-mant.meaning: A meditation on Kubera, lord of treasures: seated on his aerial chariot, radiant like Garuda, friend of Lord Shiva, crowned and jewelled, holding the boon-giving gesture in one hand and a mace in the other.

mantra:dhanteras:shri-kubera-avahana-man.meaning: Invokes Kubera, asking him to come and dwell here, and to protect and increase the worshipper's treasure and wealth.

mantra:dhanteras:shri-kubera-pushpanjali.meaning: Offers Kubera a golden, jewel-decked seat, presenting five flowers for him to be seated.

mantra:dhanteras:shri-kubera-nav-upchara.meaning: The nine-fold offering to Kubera: in turn, water for the feet, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping-water and betel — each with 'Om, salutations to Shri Kubera.'

mantra:dhanteras:shri-kubera-puja-samarp.meaning: Concludes the worship, dedicating it all to Kubera, the lord of wealth, with repeated salutations and a prayer that he be pleased.

mantra:holika:holi-pavitrikarana-mantra-.meaning: A short purification mantra invoking the lotus-eyed Lord (Vishnu) to purify the worshipper and the space.
A short purification mantra invoking the lotus-eyed Lord (Vishnu) to purify the worshipper and the space.

mantra:holika:holi-sankalpa-mantra-devan.meaning: The sankalpa, a formal statement of intent. Naming the cosmic era, place and occasion (with blanks for one's own region, name and family line), the worshipper resolves to perform the Holi worship of Prahlada, Narasimha, Holika and the others, for long life, prosperity and relief from troubles.

mantra:holika:holi-sankalpa-mantra-roman.meaning: The sankalpa, a formal statement of intent. Naming the cosmic era, place and occasion (with blanks for one's own region, name and family line), the worshipper resolves to perform the Holi worship of Prahlada, Narasimha, Holika and the others, for long life, prosperity and relief from troubles.

mantra:holika:holi-ganesha-dhyana-mantra.meaning: A meditation on Ganesha as the elephant-faced, obstacle-removing son of Uma who dispels sorrow, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to him at the start of the worship.
A meditation on Ganesha as the elephant-faced, obstacle-removing son of Uma who dispels sorrow, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to him at the start of the worship.

mantra:holika:holi-ambika-dhyana-mantra-.meaning: A salutation to the Goddess Ambika (Gauri, Narayani), the auspicious one who grants every aim and to whom devotees turn for refuge, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to her.
A salutation to the Goddess Ambika (Gauri, Narayani), the auspicious one who grants every aim and to whom devotees turn for refuge, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to her.

mantra:holika:holi-narasimha-dhyana-mant.meaning: A meditation on Lakshmi-Narasimha, seated serene in the ocean of milk, brilliant and radiant, bearing the discus and weapons, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to him.
A meditation on Lakshmi-Narasimha, seated serene in the ocean of milk, brilliant and radiant, bearing the discus and weapons, followed by an offering of sandal, rice and flowers to him.

mantra:holika:holi-prahalada-dhyana-mant.meaning: A verse honouring the devotee Prahlada, reflecting that even Brahma, the gods and the sages cannot fully praise the Lord (Hari); sandal, rice and flowers are then offered to Prahlada.
A verse honouring the devotee Prahlada, reflecting that even Brahma, the gods and the sages cannot fully praise the Lord (Hari); sandal, rice and flowers are then offered to Prahlada.

mantra:holika:holi-prarthana-mantra-deva.meaning: A prayer addressed to Holika, asking her to be a bestower of wellbeing and prosperity upon those who worship her.

mantra:holika:holi-prarthana-mantra-roma.meaning: A prayer addressed to Holika, asking her to be a bestower of wellbeing and prosperity upon those who worship her.

mantra:holika:prahlad-mantra-for-protect.meaning: The twelve-syllable salutation to Vasudeva ('Om, salutations to Bhagavan Vasudeva'), chanted for protection, as the child Prahlada is said to have taken refuge in the Lord.

mantra:holika:holika-mantra-for-burning-.meaning: 'Om, salutations to Holika' — a simple salutation to Holika, recited at the bonfire to symbolically burn away negativity.

mantra:holika:narasimha-mantra-for-victo.meaning: The opening of the Narasimha mantra, invoking the Lord as fierce, valiant, the great Vishnu, blazing and facing every direction — recited for protection and victory over evil.

mantra:krishna-janmashtami:krishna-janma.meaning: A meditation on the newborn Krishna as a wondrous four-armed child with lotus eyes, bearing the conch and mace, adorned with the Kaustubha gem and yellow silk — the form his father Vasudeva beheld at his birth.
Opening with the Vedic verse to the cosmic Purusha, this invokes Krishna together with his family — Balarama, Vasudeva, Devaki, Nanda, Yashoda and Subhadra — into the image for worship.
Offers a jewelled throne to the child Krishna, addressing him as king of kings, and asks him to be seated.
Offers water to wash the feet of the child Krishna, saluting him as Achyuta and Govinda, the lotus-eyed remover of his devotees' distress.
Offers arghya, a respectful offering of water, to Krishna, praising him as the perfect and supreme bliss.
Offers water for sipping (achamana) to Krishna, saluting him as the pure, eternal Lord who is the very form of knowledge.
Offers a ceremonial bath to the child Krishna with water gathered from holy places.
Offers garments — bright golden-yellow silk — to the child Krishna.
Offers the sacred thread (yajnopavita) to Krishna; the verse also prays for the removal of hunger, poverty and misfortune.
Offers fragrant sandal paste — with saffron, aguru, musk and camphor — to the child Krishna.
Offers ornaments set with diamond, ruby, cat's-eye, pearl, coral and topaz to the child Krishna.
Offers assorted fragrant substances to the child Krishna, with a Vedic verse invoking the Lord's all-encompassing protection.
Offers fragrant flowers and garlands, gathered for worship, to the child Krishna.
Worships Krishna limb by limb, saluting a different name of the Lord for each part of the body — the feet, knees, navel, heart, arms, throat, face, eyes, forehead, head and the whole form.
Offers fragrant incense (dhupa) to the child Krishna, together with a Vedic verse on the cosmic Purusha.
Offers a lit ghee lamp to the child Krishna, a light that removes the darkness of the three worlds, with a prayer for protection.
Offers food (naivedya) to the child Krishna, presenting it to the five vital breaths of the body with the 'swaha' offerings, and praying for steadfast devotion, grace and a desired boon.
Offers tambula (betel leaf with areca nut, cardamom and clove) to the child Krishna.
Offers dakshina, a gift of gold, to the child Krishna, praying for peace and lasting merit.
Offers the great arati lamp (mahanirajana) to the child Krishna, accompanied by verses invoking Shri (Lakshmi) and prosperity.
Offers circumambulation to Krishna; the verse says that sins of past births are destroyed with every step taken around the Lord, who is the worshipper's only refuge.
Offers salutations and full eight-limbed prostration to Krishna, bowing to him as Govinda, lord of the world, son of Devaki and Vasudeva, and beloved of the cowherds.

A closing prayer for pardon, asking Krishna to forgive the thousands of faults committed knowingly or unknowingly and to accept the worshipper as his servant; it bids the gods depart and dedicates all the worship to Krishna.

mantra:kuber-upasana:kuber-beej-mantra.meaning: A beej (seed-syllable) mantra of Kubera. Its syllables carry no sentence-meaning; they invoke Kubera as Vitteshvara, lord of wealth, closing with 'salutations to the lord of wealth.'

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kubera-dhyana-.meaning: A meditation on Kubera, lord of treasures: seated on his aerial chariot, radiant like Garuda, friend of Lord Shiva, crowned and jewelled, holding the boon-giving gesture in one hand and a mace in the other.

A meditation on Kubera, lord of treasures: seated on his aerial chariot, radiant like Garuda, friend of Lord Shiva, crowned and jewelled, holding the boon-giving gesture in one hand and a mace in the other.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kubera-avahana.meaning: Invokes Kubera, asking him to come and dwell here, and to protect and increase the worshipper's treasure and wealth.
Invokes Kubera, asking him to come and dwell here, and to protect and increase the worshipper's treasure and wealth.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kubera-pushpan.meaning: Offers Kubera a golden, jewel-decked seat, presenting five flowers for him to be seated.
Offers Kubera a golden, jewel-decked seat, presenting five flowers for him to be seated.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kubera-nav-upc.meaning: The nine-fold offering to Kubera: in turn, water for the feet, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping-water and betel — each with 'Om, salutations to Shri Kubera.'
The nine-fold offering to Kubera: in turn, water for the feet, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping-water and betel — each with 'Om, salutations to Shri Kubera.'

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kubera-puja-sa.meaning: Concludes the worship, dedicating it all to Kubera, the lord of wealth, with repeated salutations and a prayer that he be pleased.
Concludes the worship, dedicating it all to Kubera, the lord of wealth, with repeated salutations and a prayer that he be pleased.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kuber-dhyana-m.meaning: A meditation on Kubera, lord of treasures: seated on his aerial chariot, radiant like Garuda, friend of Lord Shiva, crowned and jewelled, holding the boon-giving gesture in one hand and a mace in the other.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kuber-aavahan-.meaning: Invokes Kubera, asking him to come and dwell here, and to protect and increase the worshipper's treasure and wealth.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kuber-pushpanj.meaning: Offers Kubera a golden, jewel-decked seat, presenting five flowers for him to be seated.

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kuber-navopcha.meaning: The nine-fold offering to Kubera: in turn, water for the feet, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping-water and betel — each with 'Om, salutations to Shri Kubera.'

mantra:kuber-upasana:shri-kuber-puja-sam.meaning: Concludes the worship, dedicating it all to Kubera, the lord of wealth, with repeated salutations and a prayer that he be pleased.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-nama-chanting.meaning: A simple repeated salutation to Lord Rama ('Om, salutations to Sri Rama'), chanted as japa.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-dhyana-man.meaning: A meditation on Rama, dark as sapphire with wide gentle eyes, attended by Dasharatha at his right, Lakshmana behind him holding a golden parasol, Bharata and Shatrughna at his sides with fans, and Hanuman before him seeking his grace.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-avahana-ma.meaning: Opening with the Vedic verse to the cosmic Purusha, this invokes Rama — beloved of Janaki (Sita) and son of Kaushalya — together with Sita and his whole retinue into the image for worship.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-asana-mant.meaning: Offers a jewelled throne to Rama, addressing him as king of kings, and asks him to be seated.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-padya-mant.meaning: Offers water to wash Rama's feet, saluting him as the lotus-eyed purifier of the three worlds and lord of the Raghus.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-arghya-man.meaning: Offers arghya, a respectful offering of water, to Rama, praising him as the perfect and supreme bliss.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-achamaniya.meaning: Offers water for sipping (achamana) to Rama, saluting him as the pure, eternal Lord of all the worlds who is the very form of knowledge.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-madhuparka.meaning: Offers madhuparka (a welcome mixture of honey and curd) to Rama, the husband of Janaki.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-snana-mant.meaning: Offers a ceremonial bath to Rama with water gathered from holy places.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-panchamrit.meaning: Offers a bath of panchamrita — milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar — to Rama.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-vastra-man.meaning: Offers garments — golden-yellow silk — to Rama, together with a Vedic verse on the cosmic Purusha.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-yajnopavit.meaning: Offers the sacred thread (yajnopavita) with an upper cloth to Rama; the verse also prays for the removal of hunger, poverty and misfortune.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-gandha-man.meaning: Offers fragrant sandal paste — with saffron, aguru, musk and camphor — to Rama.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-pushpani-m.meaning: Offers flowers and garlands — tulsi, jasmine, champak, lotus, kadamba, karavira and more — to Rama.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-anga-puja-.meaning: Worships Rama limb by limb, saluting a different name of the Lord for each part of the body — the feet, ankles, knees, thighs, navel, heart, throat, arms, face, teeth, forehead, head and the whole form.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-dhupam-man.meaning: Offers fragrant incense (dhupa) to Rama, together with a Vedic verse on the cosmic Purusha.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-deepam-man.meaning: Offers a lit lamp to Rama, a light that removes the darkness of the three worlds, saluting him as lord of all lights.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-naivedya-m.meaning: Offers food (naivedya) — nectar-like food of the six flavours — to Rama and Sita, followed by water for sipping.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-phala-mant.meaning: Offers fruit to Rama, praying that one's efforts bear fruit birth after birth.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-tambula-ma.meaning: Offers tambula (betel leaf with areca nut and camphor) to Rama.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-dakshina-m.meaning: Offers dakshina, a gift of gold, to Rama, praying for peace and lasting merit.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-nirajana-m.meaning: Offers arati (nirajana, the waving of light), with song, music and recitation, to Rama, praying that he be pleased.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-mantra-pus.meaning: Offers the mantra-pushpanjali (a handful of flowers with sacred verse) to Rama, saluting him as lord of the gods, the infinite Lord of pure consciousness and husband of Sita.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-pradakshin.meaning: Offers circumambulation to Rama; the verse says that all sins are destroyed with every step taken around the Lord.

mantra:ram-navami:rama-navami-kshamarpan.meaning: A closing prayer for pardon, asking Rama to forgive the many faults committed and to accept the worshipper as his servant; it bids the gods depart and dedicates all the worship to Rama.

mantra:diwali:lakshmi-chalisa-opening-ve.meaning: Opening couplet of the Lakshmi Chalisa. The devotee calls to mind Goddess Lakshmi, the ocean-born giver of blessings, praying for knowledge, intellect, and wisdom, and asking her, as the most benevolent one, to fulfill every hope.

mantra:diwali:shree-mahalakshmi-suktam-o.meaning: The opening verse of the Shri Sukta. It calls on Agni, the fire-god, to bring Goddess Lakshmi near to the worshipper: she who is golden-hued, wearing garlands of gold and silver, radiant like the moon, and the very embodiment of gold.

mantra:diwali:dhanvantari-puja-achamana-.meaning: A purification formula recited with a sip of water before worship, asking that the principle of the self, of knowledge, and of Shiva be cleansed within the worshipper.

mantra:diwali:dhanvantari-puja-sankalpa-.meaning: The sankalpa, or formal vow of intent. Naming the cosmic age, the date, the place, and the worshipper's own name and lineage (spoken where the text marks 'Amuka'), it resolves to perform the worship of Lord Dhanvantari for the sake of long life, health, and prosperity.

mantra:diwali:dhanvantari-puja-atma-shod.meaning: A common purification verse: whoever calls to mind Vishnu, the lotus-eyed Lord, becomes pure within and without, whatever pure or impure state they may be in.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-dhyana-ma.meaning: A meditation on Lord Dhanvantari, the physician of the gods: four-armed and robed in yellow, adorned with every ornament, youthful and lotus-eyed, bearing the pot of the nectar of immortality, and honored by gods and demons alike.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-avahana-m.meaning: An invocation that calls the radiant Lord Dhanvantari to come and accept the worship being offered.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-asana-pus.meaning: An offering of a seat: the worshipper presents a throne adorned with gold and jewels and lays down five flowers, inviting Lord Dhanvantari to be seated.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-swagat-ma.meaning: A brief welcome to Lord Dhanvantari.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-padya-sam.meaning: An offering formula: the worshipper devotedly presents water to wash the Lord's feet and bows to him.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-arghya-sa.meaning: An offering formula: with salutations to the Lord who upholds the earth and the universe, the worshipper offers water mixed with sandal, flowers, rice, and fruit for the ceremonial bathing of the head.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-chandan-s.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant sandal paste is presented for anointing the Lord's body.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-pushpa-sa.meaning: An offering of flowers: the worshipper honors the Lord of the world with an array of blossoms and leaves such as jasmine, champak, lotus, bilva, and tulsi, and asks him to be pleased.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-dhoop-sam.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant incense, made from the sap of trees and fit for all the gods to inhale, is presented.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-deep-sama.meaning: An offering formula: a ghee lamp that dispels the darkness of the three worlds is offered with devotion, with a prayer for the Lord's protection.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-naivedya-.meaning: An offering of food: sweets, curd, milk, and ghee are presented for the Lord to accept, offered for each of the five vital breaths of the body.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-achamana-.meaning: An offering formula: after the food, the worshipper offers water for sipping and for rinsing the hands and mouth, along with sandal paste for the hands.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-tambula-s.meaning: An offering formula: tambula (betel leaf with areca nut, camphor, and cardamom) is presented to refresh the Lord's mouth.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-dakshina-.meaning: An offering of dakshina (a ceremonial gift): a golden flower is presented, with a prayer to the Lord, giver of endless merit, for peace.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-pradakshi.meaning: Recited while circling the deity: may the sins of past lives be destroyed step by step; the worshipper takes refuge in the Lord alone and asks his merciful forgiveness.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-vandana-s.meaning: A prayer for forgiveness offered with flowers: whatever wrongs the worshipper has committed by hand, body, deed, ears, eyes, or mind, whether known or unknown, he asks the merciful Lord to pardon them all.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-sashtanga.meaning: A full prostration (made with eight parts of the body touching the ground) offered to the Lord who sustains the world and works everyone's welfare, with salutations to the infinite one of countless forms.

mantra:diwali:shri-dhanvantari-kshama-pr.meaning: A closing plea for forgiveness: the worshipper admits he does not know the proper rites of invocation, worship, or farewell, and asks the Lord to accept his imperfect worship as complete, to pardon his countless daily faults, and to grant welfare, prosperity, beauty, victory, and fame.

mantra:diwali:shri-kali-dhyana-mantra.meaning: A meditation on Goddess Kali: dark-limbed and dwelling in the cremation ground, her hands holding a sword and a freshly severed head while also granting fearlessness and boons, wearing a garland of heads and a girdle of severed arms; the worshipper bows to this fierce form.

mantra:diwali:shri-kali-avahana-mantra.meaning: An invocation of Goddess Kali. The worshipper asks the compassionate Mother, easily reached through devotion, to come with her retinue and stay present throughout the worship, to rescue him from the fearful ocean of worldly existence, and to protect his life, honor, family, children, and wealth while granting every success.

mantra:diwali:shri-kali-pushpanjali-asan.meaning: An offering of a seat: the worshipper presents a throne adorned with gold and jewels and lays down five flowers, inviting Goddess Kali to be seated.

mantra:diwali:shri-kali-navopachara-puja.meaning: A sequence of nine offerings to Goddess Kali, each made with salutations: water for the feet, water for the head, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, water for sipping, and betel leaf.

mantra:diwali:shri-kali-puja-samarpana-m.meaning: A closing dedication: salutations to Mahakali; may Goddess Kali be pleased by this worship.

mantra:diwali:deep-malika-dhyana-mantra.meaning: A meditation on the festival lamp: the lamp is addressed as the form of Brahma that destroys darkness, and the worshipper asks it to accept his worship and increase his vigor and radiance.

mantra:diwali:deep-malika-pushpanjali-ma.meaning: A brief salutation to the Deep-Vriksha, the 'tree of lamps.'

mantra:diwali:deep-malika-prarthana-mant.meaning: A prayer to the light of the lamp, honored as the Goddess: may the lamp's light bring good fortune, health, happiness, and prosperity and enlighten the mind; accepting the row of lamps offered, may the Goddess grant the vision of true knowledge.

mantra:diwali:ganesh-mantra.meaning: A short salutation to Ganapati (Ganesha), the remover of obstacles, invoked at the start of the Diwali worship.

mantra:diwali:lakshmi-mantra.meaning: A salutation to Mahalakshmi, goddess of wealth and prosperity, using her seed-syllables (Shreem, Hreem) to invoke her blessings.

mantra:ganesh:vakratunda-mahakaya-mantra.meaning: One of Ganesha's most popular verses: it praises the god with the curved trunk and mighty body, radiant as ten million suns, and asks him to keep all the worshipper's undertakings free of obstacles, always.

mantra:ganesh:om-gajaananam-mantra.meaning: A verse of praise to the elephant-faced Ganesha, attended by the hosts of spirits, son of Uma (Parvati), and remover of sorrow; the worshipper bows at the lotus feet of the lord of obstacles.

mantra:ganesh:shuklambharadharam-mantra.meaning: A widely recited verse for removing obstacles: the worshipper meditates on the one clad in white, all-pervading, moon-hued and four-armed, with a serene face, so that all hindrances may be stilled.

mantra:ganesh:ganapati-beej-mantra.meaning: The seed (bija) mantra of Ganapati: using his seed-syllable 'Gam,' it salutes Ganesha to invoke his presence and blessings.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-dhyana-mantra.meaning: A meditation on Ganesha: elephant-faced and radiant as the rising sun, robed in red, holding a noose, a goad, and gestures of fearlessness and boon-giving in his lotus hands, remover of all sorrow and beautifully adorned.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-avahana-mantr.meaning: An invocation that calls the radiant Lord Ganapati to come and accept the worship being offered.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-pushpanjali-a.meaning: An offering of a seat adorned with gold and jewels, with five flowers, inviting Ganesha to be seated.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-swagat-mantra.meaning: A brief welcome to Lord Ganesha.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-padya-mantra.meaning: An offering formula: the worshipper devotedly presents water to wash Ganesha's feet and bows to him.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-arghya-mantra.meaning: An offering formula: with salutations to the Lord who upholds the earth, the worshipper offers water mixed with sandal, flowers, rice, and fruit for the ceremonial bathing of the head.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-chandan-samar.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant sandal paste is presented for anointing Ganesha's body.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-pushpa-samarp.meaning: An offering of flowers: the worshipper honors Ganesha with seasonal flowers, bilva, and tulsi leaves, and asks for his blessings.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-dhupa-samarpa.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant incense, made from the sap of trees and fit for all the gods to inhale, is presented.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-dipa-samarpan.meaning: An offering formula: a ghee lamp that dispels the darkness of the three worlds is offered with devotion, with a prayer for protection.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-naivedya-sama.meaning: An offering of food, sweets, curd, milk, and ghee, presented for Ganesha to accept, offered for each of the five vital breaths of the body.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-achamana-sama.meaning: An offering formula: after the food, water is offered for sipping and for rinsing the hands and mouth, along with sandal paste for the hands.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-tambula-samar.meaning: An offering formula: tambula (betel leaf with areca nut, camphor, and cardamom) is presented to refresh Ganesha's mouth.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-dakshina-mant.meaning: An offering of dakshina (a ceremonial gift): a golden flower is presented, with a prayer to the giver of endless merit for peace.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-pradakshina-m.meaning: Recited while circling the deity: may the sins of past lives be destroyed step by step; the worshipper takes refuge in Ganesha alone and asks his merciful forgiveness.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-vandana-sahit.meaning: A prayer for forgiveness offered with flowers: whatever wrongs the worshipper has committed by hand, body, deed, ears, eyes, or mind, whether known or unknown, he asks Ganesha to pardon them all and protect him.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-sashtanga-pra.meaning: A full prostration (made with eight parts of the body touching the ground) offered to Ganesha, who sustains the world and works everyone's welfare, with salutations to the infinite one of countless forms.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-kshama-prarth.meaning: A closing plea for forgiveness: the worshipper admits he does not know the proper rites and asks Ganesha to accept his imperfect worship as complete, to pardon his countless daily faults, and to grant welfare, prosperity, beauty, victory, and fame.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganesha-raksha-mantra.meaning: A prayer for protection: the worshipper calls on Ganesha, lord of the ganas and protector of the three worlds who gives fearlessness to devotees, to guard him and carry him across the ocean of worldly existence; may Ganesha, with Riddhi and Siddhi, be pleased.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganapati-avirbhava-ma.meaning: The Vedic 'Ganapati' verse invoking Ganapati as the seer of seers, most renowned, the eldest king and lord of sacred speech; the worshipper calls him to come, listen, and take his seat.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganapati-avahana-mant.meaning: A brief invocation with the sacred utterance 'Om bhur bhuvah svah,' asking Ganapati to come, to be present, and to accept the worship.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganapati-prana-pratis.meaning: A Vedic verse used to invoke the divine life-force into the image, calling on Brihaspati and all the gods to be present and to make the worship complete and firmly established.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganapati-dashopachara.meaning: A sequence of ten offerings to Ganapati, each made with salutations: water for the feet, water for the head, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, water for sipping, betel leaf, and a gift.

mantra:ganesh:shri-ganapati-prarthana-ma.meaning: A short closing prayer: may Ganapati be pleased by this worship.

mantra:ganesh:ganesha-visarjan-sloka-mus.meaning: The verse recited at the farewell (visarjan) of Ganesha: salutations to the mouse-riding lord who holds a modak, has fan-like ears and a hanging sacred thread, is dwarfish in form, and is the son of Shiva; obeisance at the feet of Vighna-Vinayaka, the remover of obstacles.

mantra:ganesh:popular-ganesha-chant.meaning: A joyful devotional cheer hailing Ganesha ('Ganapati Bappa Moriya'), popularly called out during his worship and especially at his festival.

mantra:narayan-bali:dhyanam.meaning: A meditation on Lord Vishnu (Satyanarayana): the eternal Lord beyond the three qualities, master of the three worlds, dark-hued and robed in yellow, wearing the Kaustubha gem and the Shrivatsa mark, beloved of Gokula and worshipped even by Brahma.

mantra:narayan-bali:avahanam.meaning: An invocation inviting Lord Satyanarayana to come with Goddess Lakshmi and accept the worship being offered.

mantra:narayan-bali:asanam.meaning: An offering of a seat: the worshipper presents a jeweled, gold-adorned throne for the Lord to be seated.

mantra:narayan-bali:padyam.meaning: An offering formula: water is offered to wash the Lord's feet, with a prayer for the family's happiness and well-being.

mantra:narayan-bali:panchamrita-snanam.meaning: An offering of the five-nectar bath: the Lord is bathed with milk, curd, ghee, honey, and sugar.

mantra:narayan-bali:vastram.meaning: An offering of garments: fresh clothing is presented to the Lord as a gift of the worshipper's very best.

mantra:narayan-bali:naivedyam.meaning: An offering of food: sweet dishes made with ghee, milk-pudding, and sugar are presented for the Lord to bless.

mantra:narayan-bali:aarti-upachara.meaning: The aarti offering: a lamp filled with cow's ghee is waved before the Lord, asking him to look upon the worshipper with favor.

mantra:narayan-bali:arghyam.meaning: An offering formula: with salutations to the Lord of the senses, whose form is both manifest and unmanifest, the worshipper devotedly presents arghya, an offering of water.

mantra:narayan-bali:achamaniyam.meaning: An offering formula: pure water, likened to that of the celestial Ganga which removes all sins, is presented for the Lord to sip.

mantra:narayan-bali:shuddhodaka-snanam.meaning: An offering formula: pure, auspicious water gathered from many holy places, which removes all sins, is offered for the Lord's bath.

mantra:narayan-bali:yajnopavitam.meaning: An offering of the sacred thread (yajnopavita), described as fashioned by Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva; the worshipper asks the Lord to accept it and grant all desired fruits.

mantra:narayan-bali:chandanam.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant, lovely sandal paste is presented for anointing the Lord.

mantra:narayan-bali:pushpani.meaning: An offering of flowers: fragrant garlands and blossoms such as jasmine are presented for the Lord's worship.

mantra:narayan-bali:dhupah.meaning: An offering formula: fragrant incense, made from the sap of plants and fit for all the gods to inhale, is presented.

mantra:narayan-bali:deepah.meaning: An offering formula: a ghee lamp with a wick, kindled by the worshipper, is offered with a prayer that the Lord grant him happiness.

mantra:narayan-bali:tambulam.meaning: An offering formula: tambula (betel leaf with clove, camphor, and cardamom), honored by the gods, is presented with devotion.

mantra:narayan-bali:phalam.meaning: An offering of fruit: the worshipper places fruit before the Lord, praying that it bring him success in life after life.

mantra:narayan-bali:arartikya-aarti.meaning: The aarti offering: a four-wicked lamp filled with cow's ghee is waved before the Lord, asking him to look on the worshipper and grant boons.

mantra:narayan-bali:pradakshina.meaning: Recited while circling the deity: may whatever sins were committed in past lives be destroyed step by step.

mantra:narayan-bali:mantrapushpanjali.meaning: A closing floral offering: whatever leaf, flower, fruit, water, and food the worshipper has offered with devotion, he asks the Lord (Janardana) to accept with compassion; though his worship lacks proper mantras, rites, and devotion, may it be made complete, and may Vishnu be pleased.

mantra:narayan-bali:satyanarayana-aarti-.meaning: The opening refrain of the Satyanarayana aarti, hailing the Lord as the beloved of Lakshmi (Lakshmi-ramana), Satyanarayana Swami, remover of the people's sins.

mantra:narayan-bali:main-mantra.meaning: The principal mantra, 'I bow to Lord Vasudeva (Vishnu),' the twelve-syllable salutation recited throughout the worship.

mantra:narayan-bali:closing-invocation.meaning: A salutation to Lord Narayana (Vishnu), chanted in devotion.

mantra:narayan-bali:closing-mantra.meaning: A salutation to Lord Satyanarayana, the form of Vishnu as 'the true Lord.'

mantra:narayan-bali:padyam-arghyam.meaning: An offering formula: with salutations to Narayana, who carries beings across the ocean of hell, water is offered to wash the Lord's feet, with a prayer to increase the worshipper's happiness.

mantra:narayan-bali:vastram-yajnopavitam.meaning: An offering of cloth: garments that protect from cold and heat and that adorn the body are presented to the Lord with devotion.

mantra:narayan-bali:aarti.meaning: The aarti offering: a four-wicked lamp filled with cow's ghee is waved before the Lord, asking him to look on the worshipper and grant boons.

mantra:narayan-bali:katha-chant.meaning: A salutation to Lord Satyanarayana, repeated as a chant during the reading of the puja story (katha).

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dhyana-and-avaha.meaning: Opening meditation and invocation of the Goddess. The worshipper hails Durga as the auspiciousness of all that is auspicious, the granter of every aim, the refuge to be sought, and — bowing to her as Narayani — invites her to be present for the worship.

Opening meditation and invocation of the Goddess. The worshipper hails Durga as the auspiciousness of all that is auspicious, the granter of every aim, the refuge to be sought, and — bowing to her as Narayani — invites her to be present for the worship.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-asana-mantra.meaning: Offering a seat. The devotee asks the Goddess to accept a divine seat studded with many jewels and gems and fashioned of gold.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-asana-mantra-tra.meaning: Offering a seat. The devotee asks the Goddess to accept a divine seat studded with many jewels and gems and fashioned of gold.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-padya-prakshalan.meaning: Offering water to wash the feet. Water gathered with prayer from the Ganga and all sacred waters is presented for the Goddess to wash her feet.
Offering water to wash the feet. Water gathered with prayer from the Ganga and all sacred waters is presented for the Goddess to wash her feet.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-arghya-samarpana.meaning: Offering arghya, a respectful welcome-offering of water mixed with sandal paste, flowers and rice grains, which the devotee has prepared and asks the great Goddess to accept.
Offering arghya, a respectful welcome-offering of water mixed with sandal paste, flowers and rice grains, which the devotee has prepared and asks the great Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-achamana-samarpa.meaning: Offering water for sipping (achamana). The devotee asks the Mother to sip the water, to make his devotion steadfast, and to grant his heart's wish.
Offering water for sipping (achamana). The devotee asks the Mother to sip the water, to make his devotion steadfast, and to grant his heart's wish.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-snana-mantra.meaning: Offering a ceremonial bath. Milk, curd, ghee and sugar are combined into panchamrita (the 'five nectars'), which is offered to bathe the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-snana-mantra-tra.meaning: Offering a ceremonial bath. Milk, curd, ghee and sugar are combined into panchamrita (the 'five nectars'), which is offered to bathe the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-vastra-samarpana.meaning: Offering a garment. The devotee presents cloth, offered with devotion, and asks the Goddess to accept this clothing.
Offering a garment. The devotee presents cloth, offered with devotion, and asks the Goddess to accept this clothing.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-abhushana-samarp.meaning: Offering ornaments — necklace, bangles, armlets, girdle, earrings and other jewels set with gems — for the Goddess to accept.
Offering ornaments — necklace, bangles, armlets, girdle, earrings and other jewels set with gems — for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-chandana-samarpa.meaning: Offering sandalwood paste. The devotee asks the Goddess to graciously accept this fragrant sandal, described as bringing complete joy and good fortune.
Offering sandalwood paste. The devotee asks the Goddess to graciously accept this fragrant sandal, described as bringing complete joy and good fortune.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-kumkuma-samarpan.meaning: Offering kumkuma (red vermilion powder), praised as radiant and divine, for the Goddess to accept.
Offering kumkuma (red vermilion powder), praised as radiant and divine, for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-kajjala-samarpan.meaning: Offering kajjala (kohl / eye-collyrium), described as lovely and peace-giving and made from the flame of camphor, for the Goddess to accept.
Offering kajjala (kohl / eye-collyrium), described as lovely and peace-giving and made from the flame of camphor, for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-saubhagya-sutra-.meaning: Offering the saubhagya-sutra, an auspicious thread strung with gold and gems, which the devotee ties as an adornment for the Goddess.
Offering the saubhagya-sutra, an auspicious thread strung with gold and gems, which the devotee ties as an adornment for the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-sugandhita-dravy.meaning: Offering fragrant substances — sandal, aloewood, camphor, kumkuma, musk and other perfumes — for the Goddess to accept.
Offering fragrant substances — sandal, aloewood, camphor, kumkuma, musk and other perfumes — for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-haridra-samarpan.meaning: Offering turmeric. The devotee addresses the Goddess as adorned with turmeric and a giver of happiness and good fortune, and worships her with turmeric.
Offering turmeric. The devotee addresses the Goddess as adorned with turmeric and a giver of happiness and good fortune, and worships her with turmeric.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-akshata-samarpan.meaning: Offering akshata — whole, unbroken rice grains reddened with kumkuma — which the devotee presents to the Goddess.
Offering akshata — whole, unbroken rice grains reddened with kumkuma — which the devotee presents to the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-pushpanjali-mant.meaning: Offering a handful of flowers. Named blossoms such as mandara, parijata, patali, ketaki, jasmine and champaka are presented for the Goddess to accept.
Offering a handful of flowers. Named blossoms such as mandara, parijata, patali, ketaki, jasmine and champaka are presented for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-bilvapatra-mantr.meaning: Offering a bilva (bael) leaf. The bilva tree, said to be born of nectar and ever dear to the great Goddess, provides the leaf that is offered to her.
Offering a bilva (bael) leaf. The bilva tree, said to be born of nectar and ever dear to the great Goddess, provides the leaf that is offered to her.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dhupa-samarpana-.meaning: Offering incense — a ten-ingredient guggul incense blended with sandal and aloewood — presented with devotion for the Goddess to accept.
Offering incense — a ten-ingredient guggul incense blended with sandal and aloewood — presented with devotion for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dipa-samarpana-m.meaning: Offering a lamp. The devotee presents a bright, radiant lamp fed with a ghee-soaked wick to the Goddess.
Offering a lamp. The devotee presents a bright, radiant lamp fed with a ghee-soaked wick to the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-naivedya-samarpa.meaning: Offering food (naivedya) — food of four kinds, delicious and endowed with the six tastes — for the Goddess to accept.
Offering food (naivedya) — food of four kinds, delicious and endowed with the six tastes — for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-rituphala-mantra.meaning: Offering seasonal fruits — grapes, dates, banana, mango, wood-apple, coconut, sugarcane, jamun and the like — for the Goddess to accept.
Offering seasonal fruits — grapes, dates, banana, mango, wood-apple, coconut, sugarcane, jamun and the like — for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-achamana-mantra.meaning: Offering water for sipping again (achamana). The devotee asks the Mother to sip the water, bowing continually at her feet.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-achamana-mantra-.meaning: Offering water for sipping again (achamana). The devotee asks the Mother to sip the water, bowing continually at her feet.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-narikela-samarpa.meaning: Offering coconut along with orange and other fruits for the Goddess to accept.
Offering coconut along with orange and other fruits for the Goddess to accept.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-tambula-samarpan.meaning: Offering tambula, a betel-leaf roll with cardamom, clove, musk and camphor and scented with flowers, given as a mouth-freshener for the Goddess.
Offering tambula, a betel-leaf roll with cardamom, clove, musk and camphor and scented with flowers, given as a mouth-freshener for the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dakshina-mantra.meaning: Offering dakshina. To complete and fulfil the worship, the devotee places gold before the Goddess and asks her to grant its fruit.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dakshina-mantra-.meaning: Offering dakshina. To complete and fulfil the worship, the devotee places gold before the Goddess and asks her to grant its fruit.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-pustaka-puja-man.meaning: Worship of the sacred text. The devotee offers repeated salutations to the Goddess — the great Goddess, the auspicious one, Prakriti — as the sacred book is honoured.
Worship of the sacred text. The devotee offers repeated salutations to the Goddess — the great Goddess, the auspicious one, Prakriti — as the sacred book is honoured.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-dipa-pujana-mant.meaning: Worship of the lamp. A prayer for auspiciousness, well-being, health and nourishment, and for the awakening of Self-knowledge, accompanies the honouring of the lamp.
Worship of the lamp. A prayer for auspiciousness, well-being, health and nourishment, and for the awakening of Self-knowledge, accompanies the honouring of the lamp.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-kanya-pujana-man.meaning: Worship of a young girl (kumari) as the Goddess. Addressing her as the form of all, ruler of all, and embodiment of all power, the devotee offers worship to the maiden.
Worship of a young girl (kumari) as the Goddess. Addressing her as the form of all, ruler of all, and embodiment of all power, the devotee offers worship to the maiden.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-nirajana-mantra.meaning: Offering arati (nirajana), the auspicious waving of a camphor flame likened to the light of moon, sun and fire, before the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-nirajana-mantra-.meaning: Offering arati (nirajana), the auspicious waving of a camphor flame likened to the light of moon, sun and fire, before the Goddess.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-pradakshina-mant.meaning: Offering circumambulation. The devotee tells the Goddess he has carefully walked three times around her and asks her to behold his devotion.
Offering circumambulation. The devotee tells the Goddess he has carefully walked three times around her and asks her to behold his devotion.

mantra:durga:shri-durga-kshamarpana-mant.meaning: Seeking forgiveness at the close of worship. The devotee asks the Goddess to pardon the many faults he commits day by day and to be gracious to him.
Seeking forgiveness at the close of worship. The devotee asks the Goddess to pardon the many faults he commits day by day and to be gracious to him.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-achamana-keshava.meaning: Ritual sip of water (achamana) at the start of the rite, taken while invoking the name of Vishnu as Keshava: 'salutations to Keshava.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-achamana-narayan.meaning: Ritual sip of water (achamana), taken while invoking the name of Vishnu as Narayana: 'salutations to Narayana.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-achamana-madhava.meaning: Ritual sip of water (achamana), taken while invoking the name of Vishnu as Madhava: 'salutations to Madhava.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-sankalpa.meaning: The sankalpa — the worshipper's formal statement of intent, dedicating the rite for the establishment of dharma on earth and for the happiness, peace and well-being of all people.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-agni-prajwalana-.meaning: Kindling of the sacred fire, recited with the sacred utterance 'Om, earth, atmosphere, heaven' (bhur bhuvah suvah).

mantra:durga:durga-homa-ghrita-ahuti-man.meaning: Offering of ghee into the sacred fire, made with 'bhur bhuvah suvah svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-prajapati-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Prajapati, the lord of creatures — 'this oblation is for Prajapati, not for me.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-indra-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Indra — 'this oblation is for Indra, not for me.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-agni-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Agni, the fire-god — 'this oblation is for Agni, not for me.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-soma-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Soma — 'this oblation is for Soma, not for me.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-ganapati-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Ganapati (Ganesha), invoked with his seed-sound 'gam' — 'salutations to Ganapati, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-varuna-ahuti.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Varuna, invoked with his seed-sound 'vam' — 'salutations to Varuna, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-ganapati-avahana.meaning: Invocation of Ganapati (Ganesha), calling him to come and be present at the rite.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-prithvi-tattva-c.meaning: Offering of sandal paste representing the earth-element (prithvi-tattva), with the seed-sound 'lam': 'salutations to her who is the essence of earth.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-akasha-tattva-pu.meaning: Offering of a flower representing the space/ether-element (akasha-tattva), with the seed-sound 'ham': 'salutations to her who is the essence of ether.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-vayu-tattva-dhup.meaning: Offering of incense representing the air-element (vayu-tattva), with the seed-sound 'yam': 'salutations to her who is the essence of air.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-agni-tattva-dipa.meaning: Offering of a lamp representing the fire-element (agni-tattva), with the seed-sound 'ram': 'salutations to her who is the essence of fire.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-jala-tattva-naiv.meaning: Offering of food representing the water-element (jala-tattva), with the seed-sound 'vam': 'salutations to her who is the essence of water.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-durga-ahuti-mant.meaning: Fire offering (ahuti) to Durga using her seed-sound 'Dum': 'salutations to Durga, svaha.'
Fire offering (ahuti) to Durga using the seed-sounds 'Shrim Hrim Dum': 'salutations to Durga, svaha.'
Fire offering (ahuti) to Durga accompanied by a verse invoking her by her names — as Durga, the one beyond all difficulty, the essence, the doer of all — offered into the fire.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-agni.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) to Agni, the fire-god: 'Om bhuh, to Agni, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-vayu.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) to Vayu, the wind-god: 'Om bhuvah, to Vayu, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-sury.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) to Surya, the sun-god: 'Om svah, to Surya, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-praj.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) with the three sacred realms — 'Om bhur bhuvah svah, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-vish.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) to Vishnu: 'to Vishnu, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-antim-ahuti-rudr.meaning: Concluding fire offering (antim ahuti) to Rudra (Shiva): 'to Rudra, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-parshada-mantra.meaning: Salutation to the parshadas, the attendant deities of the Goddess.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-purnahuti-mantra.meaning: The purnahuti, the final full oblation into the fire, offered to Durga with the seed-sounds 'Hrim Dum': 'salutations to Durga, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-saptajihva-agni-.meaning: Fire offering to Agni invoked as the seven-tongued (seven-flamed) fire: 'to the seven-tongued Agni, svaha.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-durga-dhyana-man.meaning: Meditation seed-mantra on Durga: 'Om Hrim Dum, salutations to Durga.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-dhyana-shloka.meaning: A meditation verse (dhyana) describing the Goddess's form for the worshipper to visualise — seated on a lion, moon-crested, emerald-hued, four-armed and bearing her weapons.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-dhyanartha-aksha.meaning: Offering of unbroken rice grains and flowers for the meditation (dhyana) upon the Goddess.

mantra:durga:durga-homa-shri-durga-namah.meaning: Salutation to the Goddess: 'Om, salutations to Shri Durga.'

mantra:durga:durga-homa-shanti-mantra.meaning: The closing peace invocation: 'Om, peace, peace, peace.'

mantra:durga:durga-beej-mantra.meaning: Durga's seed (bija) mantra. 'Dum' is the seed-syllable that invokes the Goddess, chanted with a salutation to Durga as a compact invocation of her power.

mantra:durga:maha-ashtami-mantra-ya-devi.meaning: A celebrated refrain from the Devi Mahatmya, saluting the Goddess who abides in all beings 'in the form of power' (shakti): salutations to her again and again.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-1-durg.meaning: 'Durga' — The reliever of difficulties. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-2-durg.meaning: 'Durgartishamani' — She who calms deep agonies. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-3-durg.meaning: 'Durgapadvinivarini' — The dispeller of dark forces and danger. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-4-durg.meaning: 'Durgamacchedini' — She who cuts through difficulties. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-5-durg.meaning: 'Durgasadhini' — The accomplisher of the impossible. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-6-durg.meaning: 'Durganashini' — The destroyer of miseries. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-7-durg.meaning: 'Durgatoddharini' — She who uplifts one from downfall. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-8-durg.meaning: 'Durganihantri' — The slayer of fierce demons. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-9-durg.meaning: 'Durgamapaha' — She who wards off danger. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-10-dur.meaning: 'Durgamajnanada' — The giver of ultimate knowledge. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-11-dur.meaning: 'Durgadaityalokadavanala' — The fire that destroys demonic realms. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-12-dur.meaning: 'Durgama' — The mother of the universe. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-13-dur.meaning: 'Durgamaloka' — She who illuminates the dark. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-14-dur.meaning: 'Durgamatmaswarupini' — The essence of the soul. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-15-dur.meaning: 'Durgamargaprada' — The provider of the right path. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-16-dur.meaning: 'Durgamavidya' — The supreme knowledge. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-17-dur.meaning: 'Durgamashrita' — The refuge for the distressed. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-18-dur.meaning: 'Durgamajnasamsthana' — The destroyer of ignorance. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-19-dur.meaning: 'Durgamadhyanabhasini' — She who resides in deep meditation. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-20-dur.meaning: 'Durgamoha' — She who removes worldly illusions. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-21-dur.meaning: 'Durgamaga' — The one most accessible to her devotees. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-22-dur.meaning: 'Durgamarthaswarupini' — The embodiment of absolute truth. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-23-dur.meaning: 'Durgamasurasanhantri' — The slayer of the demon Durgamasura. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-24-dur.meaning: 'Durgamayudhadharini' — The bearer of divine weapons. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-25-dur.meaning: 'Durgamangi' — The one with a beautiful, enchanting form. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-26-dur.meaning: 'Durgamata' — The eternal Mother. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-27-dur.meaning: 'Durgamya' — The one who is imperceptible. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-28-dur.meaning: 'Durgameshwari' — The supreme Goddess. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-29-dur.meaning: 'Durgabhima' — The terrifying one (to evil forces). One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-30-dur.meaning: 'Durgabhama' — The passionately loving mother. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-31-dur.meaning: 'Durgabha' — The radiant light. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-dwatrinsh-naam-32-dur.meaning: 'Durgadarini' — She who tears down obstacles. One of the 32 sacred names (Dwatrinsh Naam) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-1-sat.meaning: 'Sati' — The one who gave up her life for honour. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-2-sad.meaning: 'Sadhvi' — The virtuous, sanguine one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-3-bha.meaning: 'Bhavaprita' — One who is loved by the universe. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-4-bha.meaning: 'Bhavani' — The abode of the universe. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-5-bha.meaning: 'Bhavamochani' — The absolver of the universe. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-6-ary.meaning: 'Arya' — The Goddess (the noble one). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-7-dur.meaning: 'Durga' — The invincible one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-8-jay.meaning: 'Jaya' — The victorious one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-9-aad.meaning: 'Aadya' — The initial reality. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-10-tr.meaning: 'Trinetra' — One who has three eyes. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-11-sh.meaning: 'Shooladharini' — Bearer of the trident. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-12-pi.meaning: 'Pinakadharini' — Bearer of Shiva's bow (Pinaka). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-13-ch.meaning: 'Chitra' — The picturesque one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-14-ch.meaning: 'Chandaghanta' — One having mighty bells. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-15-ma.meaning: 'Mahatapa' — One performing severe penance. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-16-ma.meaning: 'Manah' — The mind. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-17-bu.meaning: 'Buddhi' — Intelligence. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-18-ah.meaning: 'Ahankara' — One associated with the sense of self (ego). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-19-ch.meaning: 'Chittarupa' — She who is in a state of thought. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-20-ch.meaning: 'Chita' — The funeral pyre (destroyer of death). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-21-ch.meaning: 'Chiti' — The thinking mind. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-22-sa.meaning: 'Sarvamantramayi' — One who is the substance of all mantras. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-23-sa.meaning: 'Satta' — Existence itself. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-24-sa.meaning: 'Satyanandaswarupini' — The form of eternal bliss. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-25-an.meaning: 'Ananta' — The infinite one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-26-bh.meaning: 'Bhavini' — The beautiful woman. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-27-bh.meaning: 'Bhavya' — One representing future and magnificence. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-28-bh.meaning: 'Bhavya' — One filled with magnificence. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-29-ab.meaning: 'Abhavya' — One beyond fear. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-30-sa.meaning: 'Sadagati' — One who always bestows liberation (moksha). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-31-sh.meaning: 'Shambhavi' — Consort of Shambhu (Shiva). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-32-de.meaning: 'Devamata' — Mother of the gods. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-33-ch.meaning: 'Chinta' — Thought or concern. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-34-ra.meaning: 'Ratnapriya' — Lover of jewels. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-35-sa.meaning: 'Sarvavidya' — One full of all knowledge. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-36-da.meaning: 'Dakshakanya' — Daughter of Daksha. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-37-da.meaning: 'Dakshayajnavinashini' — One who interrupted Daksha's sacrifice. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-38-ap.meaning: 'Aparna' — One who ate not even leaves during her penance. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-39-an.meaning: 'Anekavarna' — One of many complexions. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-40-pa.meaning: 'Patala' — The one red in colour. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-41-pa.meaning: 'Patalavati' — One wearing a red dress. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-42-pa.meaning: 'Pattambaraparidhana' — One wearing silk raiments. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-43-ka.meaning: 'Kalamanjiraranjini' — One wearing a musical anklet. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-44-am.meaning: 'Ameyavikrama' — One of immeasurable valour. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-45-kr.meaning: 'Krura' — The fierce one (toward demons). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-46-su.meaning: 'Sundari' — The beautiful one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-47-su.meaning: 'Surasundari' — The exceedingly beautiful one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-48-va.meaning: 'Vanadurga' — The Goddess of forests. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-49-ma.meaning: 'Matangi' — The Goddess Matangi. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-50-ma.meaning: 'Matangamunipujita' — One worshipped by Sage Matanga. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-51-br.meaning: 'Brahmi' — The power of Brahma. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-52-ma.meaning: 'Maheshwari' — The power of Shiva (Maheshwara). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-53-ae.meaning: 'Aeindri' — The power of Indra. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-54-ka.meaning: 'Kaumari' — The youthful maiden. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-55-va.meaning: 'Vaishnavi' — The invincible (Vaishnavi) power. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-56-ch.meaning: 'Chamunda' — Slayer of the demons Chanda and Munda. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-57-va.meaning: 'Varahi' — One who rides a boar. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-58-la.meaning: 'Lakshmi' — The Goddess of wealth. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-59-pu.meaning: 'Purushakriti' — One taking a human-like form. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-60-vi.meaning: 'Vimala' — The pure one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-61-ut.meaning: 'Utkarshini' — The most excellent one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-62-jn.meaning: 'Jnana' — One full of knowledge. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-63-kr.meaning: 'Kriya' — Action itself. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-64-ni.meaning: 'Nitya' — The eternal one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-65-bu.meaning: 'Buddhida' — The bestower of wisdom. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-66-ba.meaning: 'Bahula' — The abundant one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-67-ba.meaning: 'Bahulaprema' — One who is universally loved. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-68-sa.meaning: 'Sarvavahanavahana' — One who rides all vehicles. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-69-ni.meaning: 'Nishumbhashumbhahanani' — Slayer of the demons Shumbha and Nishumbha. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-70-ma.meaning: 'Mahishasuramardini' — Slayer of the demon Mahishasura. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-71-ma.meaning: 'Madhukaitabhahantri' — Slayer of the demons Madhu and Kaitabha. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-72-ch.meaning: 'Chandamundavinashini' — Destroyer of the demons Chanda and Munda. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.
