mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-73-sa.meaning: 'Sarvasuravinasha' — Destroyer of all demons. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-74-sa.meaning: 'Sarvadanavaghatini' — One who slays all demons. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-75-sa.meaning: 'Sarvashastramayi' — One versed in all sacred sciences. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-76-sa.meaning: 'Satya' — Truth itself. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-77-sa.meaning: 'Sarvastradharini' — Bearer of all missile weapons. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-78-an.meaning: 'Anekashastrahasta' — One with many hand-held weapons. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-79-an.meaning: 'Anekastradharini' — Bearer of manifold weapons. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-80-ku.meaning: 'Kumari' — The maiden. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-81-ek.meaning: 'Ekakanya' — The lone girl-child. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-82-ka.meaning: 'Kaishori' — The adolescent girl. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-83-yu.meaning: 'Yuvati' — The youthful woman. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-84-ya.meaning: 'Yati' — The ascetic who renounces the world. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-85-ap.meaning: 'Apraudha' — The one who never grows old. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-86-pr.meaning: 'Praudha' — The mature woman. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-87-vr.meaning: 'Vriddhamata' — The aged mother. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-88-ba.meaning: 'Balaprada' — The bestower of strength. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-89-ma.meaning: 'Mahodari' — One with a vast belly (holding the universe). One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-90-mu.meaning: 'Muktakeshi' — One with loose, flowing hair. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-91-gh.meaning: 'Ghorarupa' — One of fierce appearance. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-92-ma.meaning: 'Mahabala' — One of immense strength. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-93-ag.meaning: 'Agnijwala' — One blazing like fire. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-94-ra.meaning: 'Raudramukhi' — One with a fierce face like Rudra. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-95-ka.meaning: 'Kalaratri' — The Goddess dark as night. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-96-ta.meaning: 'Tapasvini' — One engaged in penance. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-97-na.meaning: 'Narayani' — The Narayani, feminine power of Narayana. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-98-bh.meaning: 'Bhadrakali' — The auspicious yet fierce form of Kali. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-99-vi.meaning: 'Vishnumaya' — The magical power (maya) of Vishnu. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-100-j.meaning: 'Jalodari' — One who holds the ethereal universe. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-101-s.meaning: 'Shivaduti' — The messenger of Shiva. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-102-k.meaning: 'Karali' — The fierce one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-103-a.meaning: 'Ananta' — The immeasurable one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-104-p.meaning: 'Parameshwari' — The supreme Goddess. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-105-k.meaning: 'Katyayani' — The Goddess worshipped by Sage Katyayana. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-106-s.meaning: 'Savitri' — Daughter of the Sun-god. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-107-p.meaning: 'Pratyaksha' — The directly perceptible, real one. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:durga-ashtottara-naam-108-b.meaning: 'Brahmavadini' — One who is present everywhere. One of the 108 sacred names (Ashtottara Shatanamavali) of Goddess Durga chanted in her name-litany.

mantra:durga:navarna-mantra-chamunda-bee.meaning: The Navarna (nine-syllable) mantra, the core mantra of the Durga Saptashati. Built from seed-syllables (bija) and addressed to Chamunda, a form of Durga, it is chanted as the central invocation of the Goddess.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:vakratunda-mahakaya-.meaning: Invokes Ganesha as the curved-trunked lord of mighty form, radiant as countless suns, asking him to keep the devotee free of obstacles and grant success in every undertaking, always.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:om-gajaananam-ganesh.meaning: A meditation on the elephant-faced Ganesha, son of Uma (Parvati), served by the celestial hosts and destroyer of sorrow; the devotee bows at the lotus feet of the remover of obstacles.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shuklambharadharam-m.meaning: A verse contemplating a serene, four-armed, moon-hued deity clad in white with a gracious face, meditated upon for the calming of all obstacles.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:om-gam-ganapataye-na.meaning: A salutation to Ganapati, lord of the ganas, invoked through his seed-syllable 'Gam'.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-av.meaning: An invocation calling Ganesha -- Heramba, son of Ambika and of the three-eyed Shiva, the four-armed, serpent-adorned king of the ganas -- to come near and be present for worship.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pr.meaning: The installation (prana-pratishtha) verse, asking the life-breath and divine presence of Ganesha, together with Siddhi and Buddhi, to become firmly established in the image and remain well-seated.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-as.meaning: Offers a beautiful golden, jewel-studded throne as a seat to Ganesha, elder brother of Kartikeya.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pa.meaning: Offers water to wash the feet of the elephant-faced Lord, ever affectionate to his devotees.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ar.meaning: Offers arghya -- water with fruit, sandal, flowers and rice -- in salutation to Ganesha, the compassionate lord of the ganas.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ac.meaning: Offers water for sipping and purification (achamana) to Ganesha, remover of obstacles, revered by the gods.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-sn.meaning: Offers a bath of pure, sin-cleansing water, likened to the celestial Ganga, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pa.meaning: Offers the ceremonial bath of panchamrita -- the five nectars of milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar -- to Ganesha.
Offers a bath of milk, said to spring from the wish-fulfilling cow Kamadhenu and to nourish all, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-da.meaning: Offers a bath of curd, moon-pale and sweet, born of milk, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-gh.meaning: Offers a bath of ghee, churned from butter and gladdening to all, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ma.meaning: Offers a bath of honey, sweet and fragrant, born of flower-pollen, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-sh.meaning: Offers a bath of sugar, pressed from sugarcane and purifying, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-su.meaning: Offers a bath of fragrant, flower-scented oil to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-sh.meaning: Offers a final bath of pure water drawn from the seven sacred rivers -- Ganga, Yamuna, Godavari, Saraswati, Narmada, Sindhu and Kaveri -- to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-va.meaning: Offers garments to Ganesha -- clothing that shields from cold, heat and wind, guards modesty and adorns the body -- with a prayer for peace.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ut.meaning: Offers a fine, beautifully patterned upper cloth (uttariya) to Ganesha in devotion.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ya.meaning: Offers the sacred thread (yajnopavita), of nine strands and three plies, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ga.meaning: Offers divine, fragrant sandal paste to anoint Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ak.meaning: Offers akshata -- unbroken rice grains reddened with kumkum -- to Ganesha in devotion.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pu.meaning: Offers fragrant flowers and garlands to Ganesha for worship.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-sh.meaning: Offers soft, auspicious shami leaves, dear to Ganesha, to the lord of the ganas.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-du.meaning: Offers fresh green durva grass shoots, held to be auspicious, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-si.meaning: Offers vermilion (sindura), red and auspicious, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-dh.meaning: Offers fragrant incense (dhoop), made from tree resins and fit for the gods, to Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-di.meaning: Offers a ghee lamp that dispels the darkness of the three worlds to Ganesha, with a prayer for deliverance.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-na.meaning: Offers food -- sweets, modaka and seasonal fruit with curd, milk and ghee -- to Ganesha, with a prayer for steady devotion.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ch.meaning: Offers sandal paste blended with musk to anoint the hands of Ganesha.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ta.meaning: Offers tambula -- betel leaves with betel nuts and cardamom -- for Ganesha's mouth refreshment.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-na.meaning: Offers a coconut before Ganesha, with a prayer for success in every birth.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-da.meaning: Offers dakshina -- a gift of gold -- to Ganesha, with a prayer for peace and endless merit.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-ni.meaning: Offers the camphor flame (nirajana/arati) to Ganesha, asking him to look upon the devotee and grant boons.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pu.meaning: Offers a handful (anjali) of fragrant seasonal flowers to Ganesha in worship.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-pr.meaning: Circumambulates Ganesha, praying that all sins, known and unknown, be destroyed step by step.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-chaturthi-vi.meaning: The farewell (visarjana) prayer, confessing the devotee does not know proper invocation, worship or ritual, taking refuge in Ganesha alone, and asking forgiveness for every lapse in word or procedure so the worship may be counted as complete.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganapati-avirbh.meaning: The Vedic invocation 'Om Gananam Tva Ganapatim Havamahe', calling on Ganapati as foremost seer among seers and lord of sacred speech (Brahmanaspati), asking him to hear the devotee and take his seat.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganapati-avahan.meaning: An invocation across the three worlds, calling Ganapati to come here, be seated, and accept the worship.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganapati-prana-.meaning: A Vedic life-installation (prana-pratishtha) verse invoking Brihaspati and the assembled gods to enliven and firmly establish the deity's presence in the image.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganapati-dashop.meaning: A tenfold-offering (dashopachara) litany to Ganapati, presenting in turn water for the feet, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping water, betel and dakshina.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganapati-prarth.meaning: A closing prayer that, through this worship, Shri Ganapati may be pleased.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganesha-dhyana-.meaning: A meditation on Ganesha -- radiant as the rising sun, elephant-faced, holding the noose, goad and gestures of protection and boon-giving in his lotus hands, clad in red, remover of all sorrow.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganesha-sashtan.meaning: A full-length prostration to Ganesha, honoured as the boundless one of infinite forms, support and cause of the world's welfare.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganesha-kshama-.meaning: A prayer for forgiveness, admitting the devotee does not know how to invoke, worship or conclude the rite, asking Ganesha to accept the imperfect worship as complete and to grant well-being.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:shri-ganesha-raksha-.meaning: A plea for protection, calling on Ganesha, lord of the ganas and guardian of the three worlds, to shield his devotees and carry them across the ocean of worldly existence.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:om-vakratundaya-hum-.meaning: The short six-syllable (shadakshara) Ganesha mantra 'Om Vakratundaya Hum', invoking Ganesha as the curved-trunked lord.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-yantra-anugy.meaning: A verse asking Ganapati's permission to begin worship of his retinue, addressing him as the supreme, consciousness-filled lord fond of the nectar of immortality.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-yantra-avara.meaning: An offering of flowers to the first enclosure (avarana) of the Ganesha yantra, asking the lord who is affectionate to those who take refuge to grant the devotee's wishes.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-visarjan-slo.meaning: A parting salutation to Ganesha -- the mouse-riding, modaka-holding, fan-eared lord, son of Shiva -- bowing at the feet of the remover of obstacles.

mantra:gauri-ganesh:ganesha-visarjan-pop.meaning: A popular devotional cheer chanted at the farewell procession, hailing Lord Ganesha (traditionally with a wish that he come again soon).

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:vakratunda-mahakay.meaning: Invokes Ganesha as the curved-trunked lord of mighty form, radiant as countless suns, asking him to keep the devotee free of obstacles and grant success in every undertaking, always.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:gajaananam-ganesha.meaning: A meditation on the elephant-faced Ganesha, son of Uma (Parvati), served by the celestial hosts and destroyer of sorrow; the devotee bows at the lotus feet of the remover of obstacles.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shuklambharadharam.meaning: A verse contemplating a serene, four-armed, moon-hued deity clad in white with a gracious face, meditated upon for the calming of all obstacles.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:ganesha-beej-mantr.meaning: The seed (bija) mantra of Ganesha, a salutation to Ganapati, lord of the ganas, invoked through his core sound-syllable 'Gam'.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-dhyan.meaning: A meditation on Goddess Lakshmi seated on a lotus, lotus-eyed and bathed from jewel-studded golden pitchers, inviting her to dwell forever in one's home with every blessing and good fortune.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-avaha.meaning: An invocation welcoming the radiant Maha Lakshmi, revered by the gods, asking her to come and accept the worship being offered.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-pushp.meaning: Offers a seat adorned with gold and jewels to Goddess Lakshmi, presenting five flowers as her seat.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-swaga.meaning: A simple word of welcome to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-padya.meaning: Offers water to wash the feet of Goddess Lakshmi, with devotion and obeisance.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-arghy.meaning: Offers arghya -- water mixed with sandal paste, flowers, rice and fruit -- in salutation to Goddess Lakshmi, who adorns the lotus and bestows wealth.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-snana.meaning: Bathes Goddess Lakshmi with the waters of the sacred rivers and prays that she grant peace and drive away misfortune.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-panch.meaning: Offers the ceremonial bath of panchamrita -- the five nectars of curd, honey, ghee, milk and sugar -- to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-gandh.meaning: Offers a bath of fragrant sandal and aloe-wood paste to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-shudd.meaning: Offers a bath of pure, sin-cleansing water, likened to the celestial Ganga, to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-vastr.meaning: Offers fresh, beautiful garments to Goddess Lakshmi, mother of the world, with a prayer for renown and prosperity.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-madhu.meaning: Offers madhuparka -- a honey-and-curd mixture in a golden vessel -- to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-abhus.meaning: Offers jewelled ornaments -- bracelets, cat's-eye gems and pearl necklaces -- to Goddess Lakshmi, with a prayer to banish hunger, want and misfortune from the home.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-rakta.meaning: Offers fragrant red sandalwood paste to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-sindu.meaning: Offers vermilion (sindura), red and auspicious, to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-kumku.meaning: Offers auspicious red kumkum powder to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-abira.meaning: Offers abir and gulal (fragrant coloured powders) and scented sandal to Goddess Lakshmi for her adornment.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-sugan.meaning: Offers fragrant oils and various scented substances to Goddess Lakshmi for anointing.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-aksha.meaning: Offers akshata -- unbroken rice grains reddened with kumkum -- to Goddess Lakshmi in devotion.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-chand.meaning: Offers fragrant, delightful sandal paste to anoint Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-pushp.meaning: Worships Goddess Lakshmi with seasonal flowers, bilva and basil leaves, asking for her blessings.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-anga-.meaning: A litany worshipping Goddess Lakshmi limb by limb -- feet, knees, waist, navel, belly, chest, hands, eyes and head -- each honoured under one of her names.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-ashta.meaning: A litany saluting the eight siddhis (yogic attainments) -- Anima, Mahima, Garima, Laghima, Prapti, Prakamya, Ishita and Vashita -- worshipped as powers attending the Goddess.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ashta-lakshmi.meaning: A litany saluting the eight forms of Lakshmi (Ashta Lakshmi) named here -- Adya, Vidya, Saubhagya, Amrita, Kamalakshi, Satya, Bhoga and Yoga Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-dhupa.meaning: Offers fragrant incense (dhoop), made from tree resins and fit for the gods, to Goddess Lakshmi.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-dipa-.meaning: Offers a ghee lamp that dispels darkness to Goddess Lakshmi, presented with devotion.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-naive.meaning: Offers food -- sweets and dishes with curd, milk and ghee -- to Goddess Lakshmi, presented to the five vital airs of the body.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-acham.meaning: Offers water for sipping and purification (achamana) and for rinsing the hands and mouth, followed by sandal paste for the hands.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-tambu.meaning: Offers tambula -- betel leaves with betel nuts, camphor and cardamom -- for Goddess Lakshmi's mouth refreshment.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-daksh.meaning: Offers dakshina -- a gift of gold in the form of a golden flower -- to Goddess Lakshmi, with a prayer for peace and endless blessing.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-prada.meaning: Circumambulates Goddess Lakshmi, praying that sins of past lives be destroyed step by step, and taking refuge in her alone.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-vanda.meaning: A closing salutation asking Goddess Lakshmi to forgive any fault of hand, body, speech or mind, known or unknown, and offering flowers at her feet.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-sasht.meaning: A full prostration to Goddess Lakshmi as Bhavani, giver of every wish, asking her to be pleased and gracious.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-lakshmi-ksham.meaning: A prayer for forgiveness, admitting the devotee does not truly know how to invoke, worship or conclude the ritual, and asking Goddess Lakshmi to accept the imperfect worship as complete.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-dhyan.meaning: A meditation on Ganesha -- radiant as the rising sun, elephant-faced, holding the noose, goad and gestures of protection and boon-giving in his lotus hands, clad in red, remover of all sorrow.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-avaha.meaning: An invocation welcoming the radiant Shri Ganesha, revered by the gods, asking him to come and accept the worship being offered.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-pushp.meaning: Offers a seat adorned with gold and jewels to Shri Ganesha, presenting five flowers as his seat.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-swaga.meaning: A simple word of welcome to Ganesha.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-padya.meaning: Offers water to wash the feet of Shri Ganesha, with devotion and obeisance.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-arghy.meaning: Offers arghya -- water mixed with sandal paste, flowers, rice and fruit -- in salutation to Shri Ganesha, sustainer of the earth.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-chand.meaning: Offers fragrant, delightful sandal paste to anoint Shri Ganesha.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-pushp.meaning: Worships Shri Ganesha with seasonal flowers, bilva and basil leaves, asking for his blessings.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-dhupa.meaning: Offers fragrant incense (dhoop), made from tree resins and fit for the gods, to Shri Ganesha.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-dipa-.meaning: Offers a ghee lamp that dispels darkness to Shri Ganesha, presented with devotion.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-naive.meaning: Offers food -- sweets and dishes with curd, milk and ghee -- to Shri Ganesha, presented to the five vital airs of the body.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-acham.meaning: Offers water for sipping and purification (achamana) and for rinsing the hands and mouth, followed by sandal paste for the hands.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-tambu.meaning: Offers tambula -- betel leaves with betel nuts, camphor and cardamom -- for Shri Ganesha's mouth refreshment.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-daksh.meaning: Offers dakshina -- a gift of gold in the form of a golden flower -- to Shri Ganesha, with a prayer for peace and endless blessing.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-prada.meaning: Circumambulates Shri Ganesha, praying that sins of this and former births be destroyed at every step, and taking refuge in him alone.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-vanda.meaning: A salutation asking Ganesha to forgive all sins of hand, foot, body, speech, ears, eyes and mind, known or unknown, with a cry of victory to him, while offering flowers.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-sasht.meaning: A full-length prostration to Ganesha, honoured as the boundless one of infinite forms, support and cause of the world's welfare.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-ksham.meaning: A prayer for forgiveness, admitting the devotee does not know how to invoke, worship or conclude the rite, asking Ganesha to accept the imperfect worship as complete and to grant well-being.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:shri-ganesha-raksh.meaning: A plea for protection, calling on Ganesha, lord of the ganas and guardian of the three worlds, to shield his devotees and carry them across the ocean of worldly existence.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:lakshmi-beej-mantr.meaning: The seed (bija) mantra of Goddess Lakshmi built on the syllable 'Shreem', regarded as the core sound-vibration holding her energy of abundance and radiance.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:mahalakshmi-mantra.meaning: An invocation of Maha Lakshmi, supreme goddess of wealth who dwells in the lotus, asking her to be pleased and to bestow her grace; it joins her seed-syllables Shreem and Hreem.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:lakshmi-gayatri-ma.meaning: The Gayatri-form invocation of Maha Lakshmi, consort of Vishnu -- a meditation asking the goddess to illuminate the mind and lead one toward prosperity.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:dhan-prapti-mantra.meaning: A tantric invocation of Maha Lakshmi asking her to fill one's home with wealth and to drive away all worry and financial anxiety.

mantra:lakshmi-ganesh:ganesha-visarjan-s.meaning: A parting salutation to Ganesha -- the mouse-riding, modaka-holding, fan-eared lord, son of Shiva -- bowing at the feet of the remover of obstacles.

mantra:navratri:maa-kalaratri-avahan-man.meaning: The Navarna (nine-syllable) seed mantra invoking the Goddess as Chamunda; it is a central mantra of Durga worship, here used to invoke Maa Kalaratri.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-pradakshina-m.meaning: The circumambulation offering, walking reverently three times around the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:chandraghanta-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Chandraghanta, the third of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: A closing prayer of farewell and apology, admitting the worshipper's lack of knowledge of proper invocation and ritual and asking Ganesha to forgive any lapses and accept the worship as complete.
An offering of arghya - a respectful handful of water with fragrance, fruit and flowers - presented to Ganesha.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-dhupa-samarpa.meaning: An offering of fragrant incense, blended with guggul, sandal and aloe, to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of the ceremonial bath in panchamrita, the five nectars of milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar.
An offering of a bath with fragrant, flower-scented oil.
An offering of water for the deity to sip and rinse the mouth.
The circumambulation offering, walking reverently around the deity; the verse notes that known and unknown faults fall away with each step.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-padya-praksha.meaning: An offering of water, drawn in prayer from the sacred pilgrimage waters, to wash the Goddess's feet.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: The life-installation (prana-pratishtha) formula, praying that the vital breath and divine presence enter and firmly abide in the image of Ganesha.
An offering of a milk bath to the deity.
An offering of a fragrant flower garland to the deity for worship.

mantra:navratri:kaalratri-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Kaalratri, the seventh of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-abhushana-sam.meaning: An offering of ornaments - necklaces, bangles, armlets, a girdle and earrings - to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:chandra-moon-arg.meaning: A brief salutation to the Moon (Soma), chanted while offering arghya to the moon (Devanagari form).

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-snana-mantra.meaning: An offering of a panchamrita bath - milk, curd, ghee and sugar - to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a handful of assorted fragrant, seasonal flowers (pushpanjali) to the deity.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-kumkuma-samar.meaning: An offering of kumkum (vermilion) to the Goddess, with a prayer that she be pleased.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a garment to the deity, described as protection from cold, heat and shame, asked for in return for peace.
An offering of fragrant incense smoke to the deity.

mantra:navratri:maa-mahagauri-mantra.meaning: A meditation verse on Goddess Mahagauri, the eighth Navratri form - seated on a white bull, robed in white and pure - invoked to grant auspiciousness and joy.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of sandal paste, blended with musk, to anoint the deity's hands.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-pushpanjali-m.meaning: An offering of a handful of flowers - mandara, parijata, jati, champaka and others - to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a coconut placed before the deity, with a prayer for fruitful results life after life.
An offering of tender shami leaves, a foliage dear to Ganesha.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-dipa-samarpan.meaning: An offering of a bright, ghee-wick lamp to the Goddess, asking her to remain ever pleased.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An invocation calling Ganesha - addressed as Heramba, son of Ambika and the three-eyed Shiva, the four-armed lord of the ganas bearing his divine weapons - to come and be present for the worship.
An offering of the lighted camphor flame (aarti) waved before the deity, asking him to be gracious and grant boons.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-haridra-samar.meaning: An offering of turmeric powder to the Goddess, invoked as a giver of well-being and good fortune.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganapati-bappa-m.meaning: A popular devotional acclamation hailing Lord Ganesha, called out in celebration and at his farewell procession.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a pure-water bath, invoking the sacred rivers (Ganga, Yamuna, Godavari and others) into the water.

mantra:navratri:kushmanda-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Kushmanda, the fourth of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:shuklambaradhara.meaning: A meditation verse invoking a serene, white-robed, moon-hued, four-armed form to be contemplated for the pacifying and removal of all obstacles before worship.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of sindoor (vermilion) to the deity.

mantra:navratri:skandamata-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Skandamata, the fifth of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:navratri:brahmacharini-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Brahmacharini, the second of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of the sacred thread (yajnopavita), described as three-stranded and made of nine threads.
An offering of akshata - whole rice grains reddened with kumkum - to the deity.
An offering of pure water for bathing the deity.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-achamana-mant.meaning: An offering of water for the Goddess to sip, with the worshipper continually bowing at her feet.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of the uttariya, the upper garment or shawl, presented with devotion.

mantra:navratri:siddhidatri-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Siddhidatri, the ninth of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:gajananam-bhutag.meaning: Praises Ganesha as the elephant-faced lord attended by the ganas (Shiva's hosts), fond of wood-apple and rose-apple fruits, the son of Uma and remover of sorrow, and bows to his feet as the lord who removes obstacles.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-arghya-samarp.meaning: An offering of arghya - water with fragrance, flowers and rice - asking the Goddess to accept it and remain ever gracious.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-narikela-sama.meaning: An offering of a coconut and other fruits to the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-rituphala-man.meaning: An offering of seasonal fruits - grapes, dates, banana, mango, coconut and others - to the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-pustaka-puja-.meaning: The worship of the sacred book, saluting the Goddess as the Great Goddess, the auspicious one and Prakriti (primordial nature).

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-dhyana-and-av.meaning: A meditation and invocation of Durga, praising her as the auspiciousness of all that is auspicious, the benevolent fulfiller of every aim and refuge of the distressed, and calling her (as Narayani) to be present for the worship.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of tambula - betel leaf with areca nut and cardamom - as a mouth-freshener.

mantra:navratri:maa-kalaratri-dhyana-man.meaning: A meditation verse describing the fearsome dark form of Goddess Kalaratri, the seventh Navratri form - with a single braid, oiled body, riding a donkey, and a terrifying appearance.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-chandana-sama.meaning: An offering of fragrant sandal paste to the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-akshata-samar.meaning: An offering of akshata - whole rice grains colored with kumkum - to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:chandra-moon-arg.meaning: A brief salutation to the Moon (Soma), chanted while offering arghya to the moon.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:mushikavahana-sl.meaning: A salutation to Ganesha as the mouse-riding lord holding a modak, with fan-like ears and a hanging sacred thread, the dwarf-formed son of Shiva who removes obstacles - bowing at his feet.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-bilvapatra-ma.meaning: An offering of bilva (bel) leaves, held sacred and ever dear to the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering formula presenting a seat, imagined as a jewel-studded golden throne, for Ganesha to occupy.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-kanya-pujana-.meaning: The worship of a young girl (kanya) as a living form of the Goddess, honoring her as the mother of the world and the embodiment of all power.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-achamana-sama.meaning: An offering of water for the Goddess to sip, with a prayer for steadfast devotion and a good destiny.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-kshamarpana-m.meaning: A closing prayer asking the Goddess to forgive the worshipper's daily faults and shortcomings.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-asana-mantra.meaning: An offering formula presenting a divine, jewel- and gem-studded golden seat for the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shailputri-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Shailputri, worshipped as the first of Durga's nine Navratri forms (Navadurga).

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:om-gam-ganapatay.meaning: The core seed-mantra of Ganesha: a brief invocation and salutation to Ganapati, lord of the ganas.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-kajjala-samar.meaning: An offering of kajjala (kajal/collyrium), made from lamp-soot, to the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-nirajana-mant.meaning: An offering of the auspicious camphor flame (aarti), likened to the light of moon, sun and fire, waved before the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-saubhagya-sut.meaning: An offering of the saubhagya-sutra, an auspicious thread with gold and gems tied at the Goddess's neck, with a prayer for lasting good fortune.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-vastra-samarp.meaning: An offering of a garment to the Goddess, presented with devotion.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of water to wash the deity's feet.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-naivedya-sama.meaning: An offering of food of four kinds and six flavors to the Goddess, with a prayer for steadfast devotion.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a honey bath to the deity.
An offering of a sugar bath to the deity.
An offering of food (naivedya) - including modak and seasonal fruits, sweets and dairy - with a prayer for steadfast devotion and blessings.
An offering of a curd (yogurt) bath to the deity.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-tambula-samar.meaning: An offering of tambula - betel with cardamom, clove, musk and camphor - as a mouth-freshener for the Goddess.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of fresh durva grass shoots, regarded as auspicious and dear to Ganesha.

mantra:navratri:mahagauri-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Mahagauri, the eighth of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of fragrant sandalwood paste to the deity.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:vakratunda-mahak.meaning: A salutation to Ganesha as the curved-trunked, mighty-bodied lord radiant as a million suns, asking him to make all undertakings free of obstacles, always.

mantra:navratri:katyayani-mantra.meaning: A one-line salutation to Goddess Katyayani, the sixth of Durga's nine Navratri forms.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-sugandhita-dr.meaning: An offering of fragrant substances - sandal, aloe, camphor, kumkum and musk - to anoint the Goddess.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-dakshina-mant.meaning: An offering of dakshina (gold or money) placed before the Goddess, with a prayer that she be pleased and fulfill the worshipper's wishes.

mantra:ganesh-chaturthi:ganesha-chaturth.meaning: An offering of a lighted lamp, praised as dispelling the darkness of the three worlds, with a prayer for protection from misfortune.
An offering of dakshina, a gift of gold or money, concluding the honors with a prayer for peace.
An offering of a ghee (clarified butter) bath to the deity.

mantra:navratri:shri-durga-dipa-pujana-m.meaning: The worship of the lamp, saluting its flame as a light of well-being, health and self-awakening.

mantra:navratri:maa-siddhidatri-mantra.meaning: A meditation verse on Goddess Siddhidatri, the ninth Navratri form - worshipped by siddhas, gandharvas, yakshas, asuras and gods - invoked as the ever-present giver of spiritual attainments (siddhis).

mantra:akshaya-tritiya:.meaning: The core Vishnu mantra: 'Om, I bow to Lord Vasudeva.' Vasudeva is a name of Vishnu, and repeating this line is a simple act of surrender to the Lord who protects and sustains the world.

mantra:ekadashi-vrat-udyapan:vishnu-mool.meaning: The root (moola) Vishnu mantra: 'Om, I bow to Lord Vasudeva.' Vasudeva is a name of Vishnu; chanting it is a simple offering of reverence to the Lord who sustains the universe.

mantra:ekadashi-vrat-udyapan:ekadashi-sp.meaning: A short salutation to Vishnu: 'Om, I bow to Narayana.' Narayana is Vishnu resting on the cosmic waters, and this line is chanted throughout the Ekadashi fast to keep the mind turned to him.

mantra:ekadashi-vrat-udyapan:parana-succ.meaning: A brief salutation to the Lord in his pure, faultless form, spoken at the parana — the moment of breaking the Ekadashi fast — as a way of completing the vow successfully. (High-level gist; the exact wording is a short devotional formula.)

mantra:ekadashi-vrat-udyapan:peace-mantr.meaning: The closing peace invocation: 'Om, peace, peace, peace.' The word for peace is said three times to ask for calm on every level — body, mind and surroundings — as the worship ends.

mantra:antim-sanskar:procession-chant.meaning: The chant carried through the funeral procession, meaning roughly 'The name of Ram (God) is the only truth.' It reminds everyone that the body passes away and only the divine name endures.

mantra:asthi-visarjan:diwali-visarjan-ma.meaning: The farewell (visarjan) verse said at the end of the worship: it respectfully asks all the invited gods to depart now, having accepted the puja, and to please return again to fulfil the family's cherished wishes.

mantra:gayatri-mantra:gayatri-mantra-dev.meaning: The Gayatri Mantra, one of the most revered verses in Hinduism. It calls on the divine light of the Sun (Savitr) that pervades earth, sky and heaven, and prays: 'May we take in that adorable radiance of the divine; may it inspire and awaken our minds.'

mantra:gayatri-mantra:gayatri-mantra-rom.meaning: The Gayatri Mantra, one of the most revered verses in Hinduism. It calls on the divine light of the Sun (Savitr) that pervades earth, sky and heaven, and prays: 'May we take in that adorable radiance of the divine; may it inspire and awaken our minds.'

mantra:govardhan:govardhan-aarti.meaning: An excerpt from the aarti (song of praise) to Govardhan Hill, worshipped as a form of Krishna. It hails 'Shri Govardhan Maharaj,' picturing a crown resting on his brow, and describes devotees offering him betel leaves, flowers and streams of milk.

mantra:kaal-sarp-dosh:rahu-ketu-kaal-sar.meaning: A protective prayer against Kaal Sarp Dosha — the affliction believed to form when the planets fall between Rahu and Ketu. It asks that the troubles, ailments and dangers this condition is said to cause, including on journeys, be warded off and destroyed. (High-level gist of a traditional remedial shloka.)

mantra:kali:shri-kali-dhyana-mantra.meaning: A meditation (dhyana) verse that fixes the fierce form of Goddess Kali in the mind: she holds a freshly severed head and a sword while her other hands show the gestures of fearlessness and blessing, wears a garland of heads with wild flowing hair, dwells in the cremation ground, and is dark-limbed. The worshipper pictures this form before beginning her worship.

mantra:kali:shri-kali-avahana-mantra.meaning: The invocation (avahana) that welcomes Goddess Kali and her retinue into the worship. The devotee asks her to be present and steady for the duration of the puja, to rescue him from the fearsome ocean of worldly life, to protect his life, honour, family and wealth, and to grant every blessing and accomplishment.

mantra:kali:shri-kali-pushpanjali-asana-.meaning: An offering of a seat (asana) to the Goddess, made with a handful of flowers: it presents her a jewelled, gold-adorned seat and requests that she lovingly accept it and sit for the worship.

mantra:kali:shri-kali-navopachara-puja-m.meaning: The nine-fold service (navopachara) to Goddess Kali. Each line, ending in salutation to her, offers one item in turn — water for the feet, water of welcome, sandal paste with rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping water and betel leaf. A formulaic list of the nine ritual offerings.

mantra:kali:shri-kali-puja-samarpana-man.meaning: The closing dedication of the puja: 'Salutations to Mahakali. May Goddess Kali be pleased by this worship.' It hands the whole ceremony over to her with repeated bows.

mantra:karwa-chauth:sankalp-mantra.meaning: The vow (sankalp) spoken to begin the fast: the woman declares that, for happiness, marital good fortune and the lasting wellbeing of her children and family, she is undertaking the Karwa Chauth (Karak Chaturthi) fast.

mantra:karwa-chauth:parvati-puja-mantra.meaning: A prayer to Goddess Parvati (as the consort of Shiva): it salutes her and asks that she grant marital good fortune and blessed children to the devoted women who worship her.

mantra:karwa-chauth:karwa-donation-mantr.meaning: The verse said while giving away the karwa (the earthen pot): the woman offers a jewel-set pot filled with water or milk and prays that, in return, her husband be blessed with a long life.

mantra:maha-mrityunjaya:mahamrityunjaya-.meaning: The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra, a great healing prayer to Shiva (Tryambaka, the three-eyed one). It worships him as fragrant and the nourisher of all beings, and asks: 'As a ripe cucumber is freed from its stem, so free me from death — but not from immortality.' It is chanted for health, protection and release from fear of death.

mantra:maha-mrityunjaya:maha-mrityunjaya.meaning: The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra, a great healing prayer to Shiva (Tryambaka, the three-eyed one). It worships him as fragrant and the nourisher of all beings, and asks: 'As a ripe cucumber is freed from its stem, so free me from death — but not from immortality.' It is chanted for health, protection and release from fear of death.
