It worships him as fragrant and the nourisher of all beings, and asks: 'As a ripe cucumber is freed from its stem, so free me from death — but not from immortality.' It is chanted for health, protection and release from fear of death.

mantra:rudrabhishek:maha-mrityunjaya-man.meaning: The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra, a great healing prayer to Shiva (Tryambaka, the three-eyed one). It worships him as fragrant and the nourisher of all beings, and asks: 'As a ripe cucumber is freed from its stem, so free me from death — but not from immortality.' It is chanted for health, protection and release from fear of death.

mantra:maha-shivaratri:shiva-mula-mantra.meaning: The five-syllable Shiva mantra: 'Om, I bow to Shiva.' It is a simple, complete act of devotion to Lord Shiva.

mantra:rudrabhishek:om-namah-shivaya.meaning: The five-syllable Shiva mantra: 'Om, I bow to Shiva,' chanted here as the water and offerings are poured over the Shiva Linga during the Rudrabhishek.

mantra:shani:shiva-mantra.meaning: The five-syllable Shiva mantra: 'Om, I bow to Shiva.' It is a simple, complete act of devotion to Lord Shiva.

mantra:shiv-puran:shiva-invocation-mantr.meaning: The five-syllable Shiva mantra: 'Om, I bow to Shiva,' used to invoke Lord Shiva at the start of the recitation.

mantra:uma-maheshwari:recited-during-abh.meaning: The five-syllable Shiva mantra: 'Om, I bow to Shiva,' chanted while the sacred bathing (abhishekam) is performed.

mantra:maha-shivaratri:lord-shiva-chalis.meaning: The opening lines of the Shiva Chalisa, a forty-verse hymn of praise. They hail Shiva as the compassionate Lord of Girija (Parvati) who ever protects the good, and picture the crescent moon shining on his brow and serpent-form earrings adorning his ears.

mantra:maha-shivaratri:lord-shiva-aarti-.meaning: The opening of the well-loved aarti 'Om Jai Shiv Omkara,' sung while waving the lamp. It hails Shiva as the primordial sound Om, one with Brahma, Vishnu and Sadashiva.

mantra:maha-shivaratri:lord-mahadev-aart.meaning: The opening of an aarti to Mahadev (Shiva), praising him as true, eternal and beautiful, the Lord of all — changeless, imperishable, unborn and the inner knower of every heart — and ending with the cry 'Hara Hara Hara Mahadeva!'

mantra:maha-shivaratri:shiva-ramashtakam.meaning: The first verse of the Shiva Ramashtakam, a hymn that praises Shiva and Rama together as one. It calls on the Lord as friend and remover of the three kinds of suffering, and prays: 'Protect me, and grant me the boon of victory.'

mantra:maha-shivaratri:shri-shiva-ashtot.meaning: The opening of the Shiva Ashtottara Shatanama Stotram, the hymn of Shiva's 108 sacred names. This excerpt begins the list — Shiva, Maheshwara, Shambhu, Pinaki, Shashishekhara, Vamadeva, Virupaksha and more — each name honouring a different aspect of the Lord.

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-1.meaning: The first part of the twelve names of Ganesha, a well-known auspicious recitation. It lists names describing him — the fair-faced one, the single-tusked, the tawny one, the elephant-eared, the pot-bellied, and the remover of obstacles (Vinayaka).

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: The first part of the twelve names of Ganesha, a well-known auspicious recitation. It lists names describing him — the fair-faced one, the single-tusked, the tawny one, the elephant-eared, the pot-bellied, and the remover of obstacles (Vinayaka).

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-2.meaning: This completes the twelve names of Ganesha (Dhumraketu, Ganadhyaksha, Bhalachandra, Gajanana) and begins to promise the reward: whoever recites or even hears these twelve names...

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: This completes the twelve names of Ganesha (Dhumraketu, Ganadhyaksha, Bhalachandra, Gajanana) and begins to promise the reward: whoever recites or even hears these twelve names...

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-3.meaning: Continuing the promise of the twelve names: for the one who recites them at the start of study, at a wedding, when travelling abroad, in battle or in any difficulty, no obstacle will arise.

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: Continuing the promise of the twelve names: for the one who recites them at the start of study, at a wedding, when travelling abroad, in battle or in any difficulty, no obstacle will arise.

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-4.meaning: A very popular meditation verse on Ganesha: 'I meditate on the deity clad in white, moon-hued, four-armed and gracious of face, so that all obstacles may be stilled.'

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A very popular meditation verse on Ganesha: 'I meditate on the deity clad in white, moon-hued, four-armed and gracious of face, so that all obstacles may be stilled.'

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-5.meaning: A famous salutation to the Goddess (Durga/Gauri): 'O auspiciousness of all that is auspicious, O gracious one who fulfils every aim, O refuge, three-eyed Gauri, Narayani — salutations to you.'

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A famous salutation to the Goddess (Durga/Gauri): 'O auspiciousness of all that is auspicious, O gracious one who fulfils every aim, O refuge, three-eyed Gauri, Narayani — salutations to you.'

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-6.meaning: A verse of assurance: for those in whose heart the Lord Hari (Vishnu), the very home of auspiciousness, resides, no undertaking ever meets with misfortune.

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A verse of assurance: for those in whose heart the Lord Hari (Vishnu), the very home of auspiciousness, resides, no undertaking ever meets with misfortune.

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-7.meaning: A verse recited for an auspicious start: 'That alone is the lucky moment, the good day, the strength of the stars and the moon, the power of learning and of the gods, when I remember the feet of Vishnu, the Lord of Lakshmi.'

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A verse recited for an auspicious start: 'That alone is the lucky moment, the good day, the strength of the stars and the moon, the power of learning and of the gods, when I remember the feet of Vishnu, the Lord of Lakshmi.'

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-8.meaning: A verse of confidence: theirs is the gain and theirs the victory — how can defeat touch those in whose heart the dark, lotus-hued Lord Vishnu (Janardana) dwells?

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A verse of confidence: theirs is the gain and theirs the victory — how can defeat touch those in whose heart the dark, lotus-hued Lord Vishnu (Janardana) dwells?

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-9.meaning: An opening salutation for success in every task: bowing first to Ganesha, the guru, the sun, to Brahma, Vishnu and Maheshwara, and to Saraswati.

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: An opening salutation for success in every task: bowing first to Ganesha, the guru, the sun, to Brahma, Vishnu and Maheshwara, and to Saraswati.

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-1.meaning: A salutation to Ganesha as lord of the ganas, worshipped by gods and demons alike for the fulfilment of their desires, and honoured as the cutter of every obstacle.

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A salutation to Ganesha as lord of the ganas, worshipped by gods and demons alike for the fulfilment of their desires, and honoured as the cutter of every obstacle.

mantra:mangal-bhat:mangal-patha-mantra-1.meaning: A closing prayer for success: may the three lords of the three worlds — Brahma, Ishana (Shiva) and Janardana (Vishnu) — grant us accomplishment in all the tasks we undertake.

mantra:mangal-dosh-nivaran:mangal-patha-.meaning: A closing prayer for success: may the three lords of the three worlds — Brahma, Ishana (Shiva) and Janardana (Vishnu) — grant us accomplishment in all the tasks we undertake.

mantra:marriage:mangal-dosh-mars-remedy-.meaning: A seed-syllable (beej) mantra invoking the planet Mars (Bhauma/Mangal), ending 'salutations to Mars.' It is recited to relieve Mangal Dosha, the Mars-related affliction in a horoscope. A formulaic planetary mantra.

mantra:marriage:saturn-shani-affliction-.meaning: A seed-syllable mantra invoking the planet Saturn (Shani), ending 'salutations to Shani.' It is recited to ease the difficulties attributed to Saturn. A formulaic planetary mantra.

mantra:marriage:weak-venus-shukra-remedy.meaning: A seed-syllable mantra invoking the planet Venus (Shukra), ending 'salutations to Venus.' It is recited to strengthen a weak Venus in the horoscope. A formulaic planetary mantra.

mantra:marriage:weak-jupiter-guru-brihas.meaning: A seed-syllable mantra invoking the planet Jupiter (Brihaspati, the Guru), ending 'salutations to Brihaspati.' It is recited to strengthen a weak Jupiter in the horoscope. A formulaic planetary mantra.

mantra:navagraha-shanti:navagraha-avahan.meaning: The invocation of the nine planets (Navagraha): it salutes the Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu and Ketu, then invites them — 'come here, be seated here, and accept my worship.'

mantra:navagraha-shanti:navagraha-dashop.meaning: The ten-fold worship (dashopachara) of the nine planets. Each line, addressed 'to the nine planets beginning with the Sun, salutations,' offers one item in turn — water for the feet, water of welcome, sandal paste and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping water, betel leaf and a closing gift (dakshina). A formulaic list of the ten offerings.

The ten-fold worship (dashopachara) of the nine planets. Each line, addressed 'to the nine planets beginning with the Sun, salutations,' offers one item in turn — water for the feet, water of welcome, sandal paste and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sipping water, betel leaf and a closing gift (dakshina). A formulaic list of the ten offerings.

mantra:navagraha-shanti:navagraha-prarth.meaning: The well-known Navagraha prayer: it names Brahma, Vishnu (Murari), Shiva (destroyer of Tripura), then the Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu and Ketu, and prays that all these planets become bestowers of peace.

mantra:office-opening:ganapati-mantra.meaning: The Ganapati seed mantra: 'Om Gan, salutations to Ganapati (Ganesha).' Ganesha is invoked as the remover of obstacles for an auspicious beginning. A formulaic invocation.

mantra:santan-gopal-mantra:santan-gopal-.meaning: The Santan Gopal mantra, a prayer to Krishna for the blessing of a child. After the seed syllables it addresses him as the son of Devaki, Govinda, Vasudeva and Lord of the world, and prays: 'Grant me a child, O Krishna; I take refuge in you.'

mantra:santan-gopal-mantra:santan-prapti.meaning: A salutation to Vishnu for the blessing of offspring: 'Om, I bow to the Lord, the source from whom the whole world is born.'

mantra:santan-gopal-mantra:shri-krishna-.meaning: A seed-syllable Krishna mantra recited for the blessing of a child, addressed to Vasudeva (Krishna) in the form of the young cowherd (Gopala). A formulaic devotional mantra.

mantra:shani:shani-beej-mantra.meaning: The seed-syllable (beej) mantra of Saturn: after the seed sounds it ends 'salutations to Shanaishchara (Saturn).' It is chanted to propitiate the planet Saturn. A formulaic planetary mantra.

mantra:shani:shani-mantra.meaning: A salutation to the planet Saturn: 'Om Sham, salutations to Shanaishchara (Saturn),' chanted to seek his grace and soften his influence.

mantra:vishwakarma:vishwakarma-puja-mant.meaning: A series of salutations offered at the opening of the Vishwakarma puja, bowing to the foundational cosmic powers that uphold the world — the sustaining energy and Mother Earth among them — before worshipping Vishwakarma, the divine architect and craftsman. (High-level gist; the recited text is a short set of ritual salutations.)

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-ekaks.meaning: The single-syllable seed (beej) mantra "Shreem" of Goddess Kamala (Lakshmi). Seed syllables have no literal meaning; the syllable is repeated to invoke the goddess's energy.

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-invoc.meaning: Invocation of the main deity of the yantra: a salutation to the goddess seated on the lotus (Kamala/Lakshmi).

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-tarpa.meaning: Standard libation (tarpana) formula: "I worship and offer libation to the sacred sandals (paduka) of Shri."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-peeth.meaning: Opening of the pedestal worship (peetha puja): a salutation to all the pedestal deities that support the seat of the yantra.
Salutations, in sequence, to the nine energies of the pedestal (Peetha Shaktis), the last one placed in the centre. A formulaic list of consecratory salutations.

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-prath.meaning: First enclosure worship (pratham avarana), at the six angles of the yantra: seed-syllable salutations offered to the six limb-deities (heart, head, tuft, armour, eyes, weapon), each followed by a libation to the sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the first enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-dwiti.meaning: Second enclosure worship (dwitiya avarana), on the eight petals: salutations offered by direction to the attendant deities placed around the goddess, each with a libation to the sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the second enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-triti.meaning: Third enclosure worship (tritiya avarana), at the tips of the eight petals: a sequence of salutations to the attendant goddesses, each with a libation to the sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the third enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-chatu.meaning: Fourth enclosure worship (chaturtha avarana), in the outer square (bhupura) across the ten directions: seed-syllable salutations to the ten guardians of the directions (Indra, Agni, Yama and the rest), each with a libation to the sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the fourth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-panch.meaning: Fifth enclosure worship (pancham avarana): salutations to the weapons of the direction-guardians (thunderbolt, spear, staff, sword, noose, goad, mace, trident, lotus, discus), each with a libation to the sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the fifth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:kamala-yantra-closi.meaning: Closing line: "Thus concludes the worship of the Kamala Yantra."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-p.meaning: Opening of the pedestal worship (peetha puja) for the Shri Sukta yantra: a salutation to all the pedestal deities that support the seat.
Salutations, in sequence, to the nine energies of the pedestal (Peetha Shaktis), the last placed in the centre. A formulaic list of consecratory salutations.
First enclosure worship (pratham avarana), at the six angles: salutations to the six limb-deities (heart, head, tuft, armour, eyes, weapon), each with a libation to the sacred sandals of Shri.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the first enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-d.meaning: Second enclosure worship (dwitiya avarana), on the eight petals, using the opening line of the Shri Sukta: "Of golden hue, fawn-like, wearing garlands of gold and silver..." offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the second enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-t.meaning: Third enclosure worship (tritiya avarana), on the twelve petals, using a verse of the Shri Sukta: "Radiant like the moon, blazing with glory, worshipped by the gods, generous..." offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the third enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-c.meaning: Fourth enclosure worship (chaturtha avarana), on the sixteen petals: a seed-syllable salutation to Kamala, offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the fourth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-p.meaning: Fifth enclosure worship (pancham avarana): a fruit-of-recitation verse of the Shri Sukta — one who, pure and devout, daily offers ghee and recites the fifteen verses of Shri, longing for prosperity — offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the fifth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-s.meaning: Sixth enclosure worship (shashtham avarana): the syllables of the Sanskrit alphabet (matrika) are recited and worshipped as the very form of Mahalakshmi, with a libation to her sacred sandals. A formulaic tantric sequence.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the sixth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

Seventh enclosure worship (saptam avarana), on the middle of the sixteen petals: a sequence of salutations to the attendant goddesses, each offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the seventh enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-a.meaning: Eighth enclosure worship (ashtam avarana), in the outer square (bhupura) across the ten directions: salutations to the ten guardians of the directions (Indra, Agni, Yama and the rest), each offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the eighth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-n.meaning: Ninth enclosure worship (navam avarana): salutations to the weapons of the world-guardians (thunderbolt, spear, staff, sword, noose, goad, mace, trident, lotus, discus), each offered to Mahalakshmi with a libation to her sacred sandals.

Closing flower-offering (pushpanjali) that seals the ninth enclosure of the yantra: "Grant me my desired attainment, O compassionate protector of those who take refuge in you; devotedly I offer you the worship of this enclosure. May it be worshipped and consecrated."

mantra:das-mahavidya:shri-sukta-yantra-c.meaning: Closing line: "Thus concludes the worship of the Shri Sukta Yantra."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-sankalpa-.meaning: The ritual vow (sankalpa) that opens the worship. The devotee names the year, month, lunar day, weekday, family lineage (gotra) and name (spoken in place of the placeholders), then declares: "For the fulfilment of all my wishes, I shall perform the worship of Shri Hanuman."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-avahana-m.meaning: Invocation (avahana) inviting Hanuman's life-breath and senses to be present in the image. Fixing the mind on the lotus feet of Shri Rama, the devotee invokes the boon-giving, wish-fulfilling Hanuman.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-dhyana-ma.meaning: Meditation verse (dhyana): Hanuman is contemplated as golden-hued like the karnikara flower, praiseworthy and supreme in virtues, who leapt across the ocean — the devotee swiftly meditates on Maruti (Hanuman).

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-asana-man.meaning: Offering a seat (asana): the devotee offers Hanuman, chief of the monkeys, an excellent divine golden seat set with nine jewels.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-padya-man.meaning: Offering water to wash the feet (padya), brought fragrant in a golden pot: "Accept this pure foot-water and be pleased with me."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-arghya-ma.meaning: Offering the respectful water offering (arghya) mixed with flowers and unbroken rice: "O son of Anjani, I offer you this jewel-set arghya."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-achamana-.meaning: Offering water for sipping (achamana): addressing Hanuman as destroyer of the demons' pride and worshipped by the king of the gods, the devotee offers pure water for rinsing the mouth.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-panchamri.meaning: Bathing with the five nectars (panchamrita — honey, ghee, milk, curd and jaggery): "O lord of the monkeys, I bathe you with these."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-shuddhoda.meaning: Bathing with pure water (shuddhodaka) brought from the Ganga and other rivers in a golden pot: "O Maruti, I bathe you with it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-maunji-me.meaning: Offering the sacred girdle (mekhala) of munja grass, threefold and strung with nine gems: "O son of the wind, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-katisutra.meaning: Offering the waist-thread (katisutra) and loincloth (kaupina) of a celibate — silk, turmeric-dyed and auspicious.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-uttariya-.meaning: Offering the upper garment (uttariya), golden-hued silk: "O saviour of Janaki's (Sita's) life, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-yajnopavi.meaning: Offering the sacred thread (yajnopavita), said to grant the full fruit of all Vedic and traditional rites: "O son of the wind, wear this pure thread."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-gandha-ma.meaning: Offering fragrant paste (gandha) of divine camphor, musk, saffron and yellow sandal: "O lord, wear it upon your forehead."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-akshata-m.meaning: Offering unbroken rice grains (akshata) dyed with turmeric and kumkum, placed amid the sandal paste for auspicious splendour.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-pushpa-ma.meaning: Offering flowers (pushpa): many kinds — blue and red lotuses, water-lilies, jasmine and others, along with bilva leaves and tulsi: "O best of the monkeys, I worship you with these."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-granthi-p.meaning: Worship of the thirteen knots (granthi puja) of the sacred cord: each knot, from first to thirteenth, is worshipped with a different name or epithet of Hanuman (son of Anjani, son of the wind, the mighty one, beloved of Rama, friend of Arjuna, tawny-eyed, dispeller of Sita's grief, giver of Lakshmana's life, crusher of Ravana's pride, and others).

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-anga-puja.meaning: Worship of the limbs (anga puja): salutations are offered to Hanuman's body part by part — feet, ankles, shins, thighs, waist, tail, navel, chest, throat, hands, face, eyes, ears, head and whole body — each with a matching epithet.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-dhupam-ma.meaning: Offering incense (dhupa): divine ten-ingredient incense with guggul and ghee, kindled with fire — "O Maruti, accept this fragrance pleasing to the senses."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-deepa-man.meaning: Offering a lamp (deepa) filled with ghee and blazing bright as the white sun: "Matchless, I offer it for the completion of the vow."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-naivedya-.meaning: Offering food (naivedya): dishes carefully prepared with vegetables, cakes, lentils, sweet rice-pudding (payasam), milk, curd and ghee.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-paniya-ma.meaning: Offering drinking water (paniya): pure river water brought in a golden vessel — "O ocean of compassion, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-uttarapos.meaning: Offering the after-water (uttaraposhana), with river water, that burns up the heap of sins like fire consuming grass: "O son of the wind, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-hasta-pra.meaning: Offering fragrant water to wash the hands (hasta prakshalana), brought from the river: "O ocean of compassion, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-shuddha-a.meaning: Offering pure water for sipping (achamaniya), filled with Kaveri river water: "O Maruti whose mind rests on the footsteps of Raghuvira (Rama), accept it."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-suvarna-p.meaning: Offering a golden flower (suvarna pushpa) bright with nine jewels: "O son of the wind, I place it upon your head."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-tambula-m.meaning: Offering betel (tambula) prepared with care: "O lord, look upon this that I have arranged before you."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-nirajana-.meaning: Offering the waving of lights (nirajana / arati) in a divine vessel of countless great jewels: "O Maruti, receive this arati of my gaze as an honoured guest."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-pushpanja.meaning: Offering a handful of flowers (pushpanjali) with a Vedic verse invoking Agni as the head of heaven and the navel of the earth, the sage-king and honoured guest of all people.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-pradakshi.meaning: Circumambulation verse (pradakshina): "I am sinful, of sinful deeds, sinful by nature, born of sin; O lotus-eyed one, save me and become the remover of all my sins."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-namaskara.meaning: Salutation (namaskara): "Salutations to you, O great hero, O delight of the wind; look upon me always with compassion and save me, O lover of your devotees."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-doraka-gr.meaning: Taking up the protective cord (doraka): those who long for children, grandchildren and every good fortune, having worshipped the son of the wind, tie this thirteen-knotted sacred cord upon the hand.

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-purvadora.meaning: Removing the previous year's cord (purva-doraka uttarana): "O one born of Anjani's womb, born to accomplish Rama's work, by the power of the sacred cord protect me year after year."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-prarthana.meaning: Prayer (prarthana): "By this worship may Lord Hanuman, the embodiment of accomplishing every task, be pleased and, being prayed to, dwell within my heart."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-vayana-da.meaning: Giving the ritual gift (vayana dana): "He by the remembrance and utterance of whose name all austerities, sacrifices and rites reach their completion — to that imperishable one I bow."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:hanuman-vayana-gr.meaning: Receiving the ritual gift (vayana grahana): "Hanuman himself both gives and receives on our behalf; for the completion of this vow, may he accept the vayana."

mantra:hanuman-chalisa:ram-naam.meaning: The Ram Naam chant "Sri Ram, Jai Ram, Jai Jai Ram" — a simple repeated invocation glorifying and hailing Lord Rama.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-sankalp.meaning: The ritual vow (sankalpa) that opens the worship. The devotee names the year, month, lunar day, weekday, family lineage (gotra) and name (spoken in place of the placeholders), then declares: "For the fulfilment of all my wishes, I shall perform the worship of Shri Hanuman."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-avahana.meaning: Invocation (avahana) inviting Hanuman's life-breath and senses to be present in the image. Fixing the mind on the lotus feet of Shri Rama, the devotee invokes the boon-giving, wish-fulfilling Hanuman.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-dhyana-.meaning: Meditation verse (dhyana): Hanuman is contemplated as golden-hued like the karnikara flower, praiseworthy and supreme in virtues, who leapt across the ocean — the devotee swiftly meditates on Maruti (Hanuman).

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-asana-m.meaning: Offering a seat (asana): the devotee offers Hanuman, chief of the monkeys, an excellent divine golden seat set with nine jewels.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-padya-m.meaning: Offering water to wash the feet (padya), brought fragrant in a golden pot: "Accept this pure foot-water and be pleased with me."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-arghya-.meaning: Offering the respectful water offering (arghya) mixed with flowers and unbroken rice: "O son of Anjani, I offer you this jewel-set arghya."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-achaman.meaning: Offering water for sipping (achamana): addressing Hanuman as destroyer of the demons' pride and worshipped by the king of the gods, the devotee offers pure water for rinsing the mouth.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-pancham.meaning: Bathing with the five nectars (panchamrita — honey, ghee, milk, curd and jaggery): "O lord of the monkeys, I bathe you with these."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-shuddho.meaning: Bathing with pure water (shuddhodaka) brought from the Ganga and other rivers in a golden pot: "O Maruti, I bathe you with it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-maunji-.meaning: Offering the sacred girdle (mekhala) of munja grass, threefold and strung with nine gems: "O son of the wind, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-katisut.meaning: Offering the waist-thread (katisutra) and loincloth (kaupina) of a celibate — silk, turmeric-dyed and auspicious.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-uttariy.meaning: Offering the upper garment (uttariya), golden-hued silk: "O saviour of Janaki's (Sita's) life, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-yajnopa.meaning: Offering the sacred thread (yajnopavita), said to grant the full fruit of all Vedic and traditional rites: "O son of the wind, wear this pure thread."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-gandha-.meaning: Offering fragrant paste (gandha) of divine camphor, musk, saffron and yellow sandal: "O lord, wear it upon your forehead."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-akshata.meaning: Offering unbroken rice grains (akshata) dyed with turmeric and kumkum, placed amid the sandal paste for auspicious splendour.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-pushpa-.meaning: Offering flowers (pushpa): many kinds — blue and red lotuses, water-lilies, jasmine and others, along with bilva leaves and tulsi: "O best of the monkeys, I worship you with these."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-granthi.meaning: Worship of the thirteen knots (granthi puja) of the sacred cord: each knot, from first to thirteenth, is worshipped with a different name or epithet of Hanuman (son of Anjani, son of the wind, the mighty one, beloved of Rama, friend of Arjuna, tawny-eyed, dispeller of Sita's grief, giver of Lakshmana's life, crusher of Ravana's pride, and others). (Devanagari text.)

Worship of the thirteen knots (granthi puja) of the sacred cord: each knot, from first to thirteenth, is worshipped with a different name or epithet of Hanuman (son of Anjani, son of the wind, the mighty one, beloved of Rama, friend of Arjuna, tawny-eyed, dispeller of Sita's grief, giver of Lakshmana's life, crusher of Ravana's pride, and others). (Roman transliteration of the same.)

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-anga-pu.meaning: Worship of the limbs (anga puja): salutations are offered to Hanuman's body part by part — feet, ankles, shins, thighs, waist, tail, navel, chest, throat, hands, face, eyes, ears, head and whole body — each with a matching epithet. (Devanagari text.)

Worship of the limbs (anga puja): salutations are offered to Hanuman's body part by part — feet, ankles, shins, thighs, waist, tail, navel, chest, throat, hands, face, eyes, ears, head and whole body — each with a matching epithet. (Roman transliteration of the same.)

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-dhupam-.meaning: Offering incense (dhupa): divine ten-ingredient incense with guggul and ghee, kindled with fire — "O Maruti, accept this fragrance pleasing to the senses."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-deepa-m.meaning: Offering a lamp (deepa) filled with ghee and blazing bright as the white sun: "Matchless, I offer it for the completion of the vow."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-naivedy.meaning: Offering food (naivedya): dishes carefully prepared with vegetables, cakes, lentils, sweet rice-pudding (payasam), milk, curd and ghee.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-paniya-.meaning: Offering drinking water (paniya): pure river water brought in a golden vessel — "O ocean of compassion, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-uttarap.meaning: Offering the after-water (uttaraposhana), with river water, that burns up the heap of sins like fire consuming grass: "O son of the wind, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-hasta-p.meaning: Offering fragrant water to wash the hands (hasta prakshalana), brought from the river: "O ocean of compassion, accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-shuddha.meaning: Offering pure water for sipping (achamaniya), filled with Kaveri river water: "O Maruti whose mind rests on the footsteps of Raghuvira (Rama), accept it."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-suvarna.meaning: Offering a golden flower (suvarna pushpa) bright with nine jewels: "O son of the wind, I place it upon your head."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-tambula.meaning: Offering betel (tambula) prepared with care: "O lord, look upon this that I have arranged before you."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-nirajan.meaning: Offering the waving of lights (nirajana / arati) in a divine vessel of countless great jewels: "O Maruti, receive this arati of my gaze as an honoured guest."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-pushpan.meaning: Offering a handful of flowers (pushpanjali) with a Vedic verse invoking Agni as the head of heaven and the navel of the earth, the sage-king and honoured guest of all people.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-pradaks.meaning: Circumambulation verse (pradakshina): "I am sinful, of sinful deeds, sinful by nature, born of sin; O lotus-eyed one, save me and become the remover of all my sins."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-namaska.meaning: Salutation (namaskara): "Salutations to you, O great hero, O delight of the wind; look upon me always with compassion and save me, O lover of your devotees."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-doraka-.meaning: Taking up the protective cord (doraka): those who long for children, grandchildren and every good fortune, having worshipped the son of the wind, tie this thirteen-knotted sacred cord upon the hand.

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-purvado.meaning: Removing the previous year's cord (purva-doraka uttarana): "O one born of Anjani's womb, born to accomplish Rama's work, by the power of the sacred cord protect me year after year."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-prartha.meaning: Prayer (prarthana): "By this worship may Lord Hanuman, the embodiment of accomplishing every task, be pleased and, being prayed to, dwell within my heart."

mantra:hanuman-janmotsav:hanuman-vayana-.meaning: Giving the ritual gift (vayana dana): "He by the remembrance and utterance of whose name all austerities, sacrifices and rites reach their completion — to that imperishable one I bow."
Receiving the ritual gift (vayana grahana): "Hanuman himself both gives and receives on our behalf; for the completion of this vow, may he accept the vayana."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-vidya-mantra.meaning: "Salutations to you, O Saraswati, boon-giver who takes whatever form is wished for; I am beginning my studies — may success always be mine." Recited before starting to learn.

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-beej-mantra.meaning: The seed (beej) mantra of Saraswati, built on her seed-syllable "Aim": a salutation used to invoke the goddess. Seed syllables carry no literal translation.

mantra:saraswati:the-saraswati-mantra.meaning: "O greatly blessed Saraswati, embodiment of knowledge, lotus-eyed goddess of learning — grant me knowledge; salutations to you."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-dhyan-mantra.meaning: A meditation on Saraswati beheld bearing the veena and a book and borne by the swan: the devotee prays that she grant the gift of knowledge.

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-puranokta-man.meaning: "To the Goddess who abides in all beings in the form of knowledge — salutations, salutations, again and again." A verse from the Devi Mahatmya.

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-gaaytri-mantr.meaning: The Saraswati Gayatri: "We contemplate the goddess of speech, we meditate upon her; may that goddess inspire us."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-vandana.meaning: The opening of the well-known Vandana: "She who is white as the jasmine, the moon and a garland of snow, clad in pure white, her hands adorned with the veena, seated upon a white lotus..."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-dhyana-m.meaning: The full meditation verse (Ya Kundendu...): "She who is fair as jasmine, moon and snow, robed in white, veena in hand, seated on a white lotus, ever revered by Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva and the gods — may that Goddess Saraswati, remover of all dullness, protect me." Offered as the meditation.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-avahana-.meaning: Invocation (avahana): opening with the Purusha Sukta ("The Cosmic Being of a thousand heads..."), then "Come, O Goddess Saraswati, and be steady in this place; remain present as long as I perform the worship."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-asana-ma.meaning: Offering a seat (asana): the devotee offers the goddess a lovely, divine seat that grants every comfort.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-padya-ma.meaning: Offering water to wash the feet (padya): pure Ganga water, fragrant with every scent, is offered for the goddess's feet.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-arghya-m.meaning: Offering the respectful water offering (arghya) with sandal, flowers and rice: "O goddess, show me your compassion; accept this arghya, salutations to you."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-achamani.meaning: Offering water for sipping (achamana): pure, fragrant water gathered from all the sacred fords (tirthas) is offered for rinsing the mouth.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-snana-ma.meaning: Offering a bath (snana) with the waters of the Ganga, Saraswati, Reva, Payoshni and Narmada rivers: "O goddess, I have bathed you; so grant me peace."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-panchamr.meaning: Bathing with the five nectars (panchamrita — milk, curd, ghee, honey and sugar): "I have brought this panchamrita; accept it for your bath."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-shuddhod.meaning: The pure-water bath (shuddhodaka snana) following the panchamrita: addressing her as the embodiment of knowledge, Bhadrakali and Narayani, the devotee offers a bath of clean water.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-gandha-s.meaning: A scented bath (gandha snana) with sandal water: sandal and aloe from the Malaya mountains are offered for the goddess's bath.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-vastra-m.meaning: Offering a garment (vastra): a fine woven cloth, skilfully made, is offered for the goddess to wear.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-chandana.meaning: Offering sandal paste (chandana): divine, fragrant Shrikhanda sandal is offered as an anointing for the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-saubhagy.meaning: Offering the tokens of auspiciousness (saubhagya dravya): betel leaf, turmeric, kumkum, collyrium, sindoor and alta are offered to the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-alankara.meaning: Offering ornaments (alankara): jewelled bracelets, armlets, a girdle, earrings, anklets, a pearl necklace and a crown are offered.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-pushpa-m.meaning: Offering flowers (pushpa): fragrant flowers and garlands the devotee has gathered are offered to the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-dhupa-ma.meaning: Offering incense (dhupa): fine fragrant incense born of plant resins is offered for the goddess to enjoy.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-deepa-ma.meaning: Offering a lamp (deepa): a wick-lamp lit with ghee, which dispels the darkness of the three worlds, is shown to the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-naivedya.meaning: Offering food (naivedya): sweet, excellent fare made with sugar and ghee, together with other dishes, is offered to the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-achamani.meaning: Offering water for sipping after the food (achamaniya): water scented with cardamom, clove and camphor is offered for rinsing the mouth.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-tambula-.meaning: Offering betel (tambula): fine areca nut with betel leaf and cardamom powder is offered to the goddess.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-dakshina.meaning: Offering the ritual fee (dakshina): a golden token (hiranyagarbha) said to yield endless merit is offered — "grant me peace."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-arartiky.meaning: Offering the camphor light (arati / arartikya): kindled camphor is waved before the goddess — "behold me and be my boon-giver."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-pradaksh.meaning: Circumambulation verse (pradakshina), which recalls the prescribed number of circuits for different deities (for example one for Chandi, seven for Surya, three for Vinayaka, four for Vishnu, and half a circuit for Shiva).

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-pushpanj.meaning: Offering a handful of flowers (pushpanjali): fragrant seasonal flowers are offered to the goddess with a salutation.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-sashtang.meaning: Full prostration (sashtanga pranama, touching the ground with eight limbs), with verses saluting the Goddess as Mahadevi, Prakriti, Durga, Kalaratri, Saraswati, Aditi and daughter of Daksha — drawn from the Devi hymns.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-avahana-.meaning: Invocation, Diwali variant (avahana): "Come, O Goddess of goddesses, radiant Saraswati! Accept the worship I perform, O one revered by the gods."

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-pushpanj.meaning: Seat offering, Diwali variant: a gem-studded, gold-adorned seat is offered to the goddess, with five flowers presented for the seat.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-nav-upch.meaning: The nine-fold offering (nava upachara), Diwali variant: a sequence of salutations offering foot-water, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sip-water and betel to Saraswati.

mantra:saraswati:shri-saraswati-puja-sam.meaning: Dedication of the worship (samarpan), Diwali variant: "Salutations to Saraswati; by this worship may Goddess Saraswati be pleased; salutations, again and again."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-dhyana-mantra.meaning: The meditation verse (Ya Kundendu...) in Roman: "She who is fair as jasmine, moon and snow, robed in white, veena in hand, seated on a white lotus, ever revered by Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva and the gods — may that Goddess Saraswati, remover of all dullness, protect me."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-avahan-mantra.meaning: Invocation (avahana) in Roman: "Come, O Goddess of goddesses, radiant Saraswati! Accept the worship I perform, O one revered by the gods."

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-pushpanjali-1.meaning: Seat offering (asana) in Roman: a gem-studded, gold-adorned seat is offered to the goddess, with five flowers presented for the seat.

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-pushpanjali-2.meaning: The nine-fold offering (nava upachara) in Roman: a sequence of salutations offering foot-water, arghya, sandal and rice, flowers, incense, lamp, food, sip-water and betel to Saraswati.

mantra:saraswati:saraswati-pushpanjali-3.meaning: Dedication of the worship (samarpan) in Roman: "Salutations to Saraswati; by this worship may Goddess Saraswati be pleased; salutations, again and again."
